# AutoResearch 2026-06-09: Source Retention Guide

## Iteration 449 - Prompt To Artifact Completion Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 448 closed deterministic GitHub branch URL end-to-end study evidence.
At this point the remaining completion risk is requirement drift: the original
goal says the app must become a vibe-coder learning app that works through both
CLI/Codex Skill invocation and direct app use. Individual smoke scripts covered
those paths, but no single lightweight check mapped the prompt requirements to
the verifier and documentation evidence.

### Candidate Adopted

`prompt to artifact completion smoke`

Add a completion map verifier that reads the existing evidence files and fails
if the prompt requirements are no longer backed by concrete artifacts. This is
a meta gate, not a replacement for the heavy entrypoint smoke.

### App Changes

- `scripts/verify-goal-completion-smoke.mjs`
  - maps prompt requirements to evidence files for vibe-coding mission,
    source input modes, CLI/Codex Skill use, direct app use, generated
    artifacts, Codex default fail-closed logs, source safety, and entrypoint
    parity
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - adds `goal-completion-map` to the user entrypoint parity gate
- `package.json`
  - adds `verify:goal-completion`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 449`
- `README.md`
  - documents `pnpm verify:goal-completion`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the 449 prompt-to-artifact completion smoke check
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 449 for handoff and resume continuity

### Verification

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-goal-completion-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
- PASS: `pnpm verify:goal-completion`
- PASS: `pnpm verify:entrypoints`
- PASS: `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `pnpm audit:brief`

## Iteration 448 - GitHub Study End-to-End Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 447 closed SKILL.md folder and CLI-Anything target end-to-end study
evidence. GitHub URL intake was still weaker: parser coverage proved URL
classification, but the clone path was not covered by a deterministic full
study smoke. Inspecting the clone path also exposed a branch argument order bug:
`--branch` was inserted immediately after `--depth`, producing an invalid
`git clone --depth --branch <branch> 1 ...` command for branch URLs.

### Candidate Adopted

`github study end-to-end smoke`

Add a deterministic GitHub branch URL study smoke using a temporary `git` stub.
The smoke avoids network dependency, verifies `git clone --depth 1 --branch
<branch> ...` argument order, copies a static fixture, runs the shared core
`runStudy` pipeline with Codex disabled, and checks generated artifacts plus
secret-like `.env` exclusion.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/storage.ts`
  - fixes GitHub branch clone arguments to `git clone --depth 1 --branch <branch> ...`
- `packages/core/src/github-study.test.ts`
  - uses a temporary `git` stub to avoid network dependency
  - verifies GitHub branch URL metadata, clone argument order, generated session
    `source/`, secret-like `.env` exclusion, quiz output, HTML output, and
    `verifyStudySessionArtifacts`
- `scripts/verify-github-study-smoke.mjs`
  - runs the GitHub study test and emits JSON evidence for clone, artifacts,
    safety, and source retention
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - includes `github-study` in the objective-level entrypoint gate
- `README.md`
  - documents `pnpm verify:github-study`
- `package.json`, `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - add the verifier script and iteration 448 audit coverage
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 448 for handoff and resume continuity

### Verification

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-github-study-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
- PASS: `pnpm verify:github-study`
- PASS: `pnpm verify:entrypoints`
- PASS: `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `pnpm audit:brief`

## Iteration 447 - Skill and CLI-Anything Study Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 446 closed ZIP end-to-end study evidence. SKILL.md folders and
CLI-Anything targets were still parser-level only: classification proved the
input mode, but not that these advertised modes can produce complete study
sessions while preserving the no-target-command-execution boundary.

### Candidate Adopted

`skill and cli-anything study smoke`

Add a deterministic special source-mode smoke for SKILL.md folders and
CLI-Anything `agent-harness` folders. The test creates temporary local fixtures,
runs the shared core `runStudy` pipeline with Codex disabled, verifies generated
study artifacts, and checks a CLI-Anything harness execution sentinel is not
created.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/source-mode-study.test.ts`
  - creates temporary SKILL.md and CLI-Anything target fixtures
  - runs `runStudy` for both source modes
  - verifies source-mode session metadata, generated session `source/` files,
    quiz output, HTML output, and `verifyStudySessionArtifacts`
  - checks a CLI-Anything execution sentinel is not created
- `scripts/verify-source-mode-study-smoke.mjs`
  - runs the special source-mode test and emits JSON evidence for artifacts,
    source retention, and no analyzed-project command execution
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - includes `source-mode-study` in the objective-level entrypoint gate
- `README.md`
  - documents `pnpm verify:source-mode-study`
- `package.json`, `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - add the verifier script and iteration 447 audit coverage
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 447 for handoff and resume continuity

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-source-mode-study-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`

## Iteration 446 - ZIP Study End-to-End Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 445 proved parser-level coverage for GitHub URL, local folder, ZIP
path, SKILL.md folder, and CLI-Anything targets. The next gap was ZIP
end-to-end evidence: source classification alone did not prove a ZIP input can
extract into a generated session `source/` snapshot and produce the required
study artifacts.

### Candidate Adopted

`zip study end-to-end smoke`

Add a deterministic ZIP study smoke that builds a temporary static ZIP fixture,
runs the shared core `runStudy` pipeline with Codex disabled, and verifies the
generated session artifacts. The smoke does not execute analyzed-project
commands; it only creates a local ZIP fixture, extracts it through the existing
core intake path, and checks artifact completeness plus secret-like `.env`
exclusion.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/zip-study.test.ts`
  - creates a temporary zipped project fixture and runs `runStudy` on the ZIP
  - verifies ZIP session metadata, extracted generated session `source/`,
    secret-like `.env` exclusion, quiz output, HTML output, Markdown output,
    and `verifyStudySessionArtifacts`
- `scripts/verify-zip-study-smoke.mjs`
  - runs the ZIP study test and emits JSON evidence for extraction, artifacts,
    safety, and source retention
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - includes `zip-study` in the objective-level entrypoint gate
- `README.md`
  - documents `pnpm verify:zip-study`
- `package.json`, `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - add the verifier script and iteration 446 audit coverage
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 446 for handoff and resume continuity

## Iteration 444 - Desktop Bundled Rust Sidecar Resource Discovery Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 443 proved the packaged sidecar resource itself can run offline and
Codex-enabled fail-closed studies. That was still one step short of the desktop
app's direct path: the Rust command discovery smoke only proved
`REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`-free discovery of the repo-local
`apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar-dist/...` bridge, while a standalone `.app` must
discover `Contents/Resources/sidecar-dist/sidecar/bridge.js`.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop bundled rust sidecar resource discovery smoke`

Add a macOS `.app` resource discovery smoke for the Rust command handlers. The
test builds the app bundle, points the Rust resolver at the app binary layout
through a test-only current-exe override, unsets `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`, and
requires `study_source`, `list_sessions`, and `resume_session` to use the
bundled resource sidecar.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - routes default sidecar candidate generation through `current_exe_path`
  - adds test-only `REPOTUTOR_TEST_CURRENT_EXE` support
  - adds `tauri_commands_discover_app_resource_sidecar_without_env`
- `scripts/verify-desktop-bundled-rust-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
  - builds the `.app` bundle
  - asserts `Contents/Resources/sidecar-dist/sidecar/bridge.js` exists
  - runs the ignored Rust app-resource discovery command smoke
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - adds `desktop-bundled-rust-sidecar` to the objective-level entrypoint gate
- `README.md`
  - documents `pnpm verify:desktop-bundled-rust-sidecar`
- `package.json`, `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - add the verifier script and iteration 444 audit coverage
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 444 for handoff and resume continuity

### Verification

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-desktop-bundled-rust-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
- PASS: `cargo check --manifest-path apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/Cargo.toml`

## Iteration 443 - Desktop Bundled Codex-Enabled Fail-Closed Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 442 proved the `.app` bundle contains a self-contained sidecar
resource and that the packaged resource can run offline deterministic
study/list/resume. The remaining desktop gap was the app's default path:
the UI sends `enableCodex: true`, so the packaged resource also needs proof
that a Codex SDK attempt does not break the local study when the SDK is
unavailable.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop bundled codex enabled fail closed smoke`

Extend the bundled sidecar smoke to run a second packaged-resource study with
`enableCodex: true`, assert the SDK path is attempted, assert Codex prompt and
event logs are written, and require the study session to complete with source
snapshots preserved even if the SDK attempt fails.

### App Changes

- `scripts/verify-desktop-bundled-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
  - adds a packaged `.app` resource study with `enableCodex: true`
  - asserts study completion, verification success, and retained source output
  - asserts `codex/prompts.jsonl` and `codex/events.jsonl` exist
  - rejects a skipped Codex path and accepts only `success` or fail-closed
    `failed` Codex event statuses
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - records the bundled desktop sidecar as covering the Codex-enabled
    fail-closed path
- `README.md`
  - documents the bundled desktop smoke as packaged proof for default
    Codex-enabled logging and completion behavior
- `package.json`, `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - move `audit:brief` and audit evidence checks to iteration 443
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 443 for handoff and resume continuity

### Verification

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-desktop-bundled-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
- PASS: `pnpm verify:desktop-bundled-sidecar`
- PASS: `pnpm verify:entrypoints`

## Iteration 442 - Desktop Bundled Sidecar Resource Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 441 proved Tauri command handlers can discover the compiled desktop
sidecar without `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`, but the compiled bridge still imported
`@repotutor/core` and sibling workspace packages at runtime. That works from the
repo checkout with workspace `node_modules`, but it is weak evidence for a
standalone `.app` bundle.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop bundled sidecar resource smoke`

Bundle the desktop sidecar into a self-contained Node ESM file, include it as a
Tauri resource, and run the `.app` resource copy directly through the JSONL
bridge to prove study/list/resume still work with generated source snapshots
preserved.

### App Changes

- `scripts/build-desktop-sidecar-bundle.mjs`
  - uses the repo-local esbuild API instead of the broken native-bin shim
  - bundles RepoTutor workspace packages into `sidecar-dist/sidecar/bridge.js`
  - leaves `@openai/codex-sdk` external so Codex SDK remains optional/fail-closed
  - rejects unresolved `@repotutor/*` runtime imports
- `apps/desktop-tauri/package.json`
  - runs the sidecar bundle builder during desktop builds
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`
  - maps the bundled sidecar into Tauri `bundle.resources`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - includes macOS app resource sidecar candidates
- `scripts/verify-desktop-bundled-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
  - runs `tauri build --bundles app --ci`
  - asserts `RepoTutor Studio.app` contains the sidecar resource
  - runs the bundled resource sidecar through study/list/resume
- existing desktop sidecar smokes
  - use the esbuild bundle builder instead of tsc-only output
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - includes `desktop-bundled-sidecar` in the objective-level entrypoint gate
- `package.json`, `README.md`, `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - add `verify:desktop-bundled-sidecar` and iteration 442 audit coverage
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 442 for handoff and resume continuity

- PASS: `node --check scripts/build-desktop-sidecar-bundle.mjs`

## Iteration 441 - Desktop Sidecar Default Discovery Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 440 proved the Tauri app shell can produce a release binary, but the
Rust command handlers still depended on `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR` to use the compiled
desktop sidecar. If that env var was absent, command handling could fall back to
a global `repo-tutor` binary instead of proving direct app use through the
compiled sidecar.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop sidecar default discovery smoke`

Resolve an explicitly configured `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR` first, then discover the
compiled default sidecar at `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar-dist/sidecar/bridge.js`
from repo, package, or current-executable-relative layouts.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - adds `sidecar_path_override`, `resolve_sidecar_path`, and
    `default_sidecar_candidates`
  - keeps explicit `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR` override behavior
  - adds `tauri_commands_discover_compiled_bridge_without_env`
- `scripts/verify-desktop-sidecar-discovery-smoke.mjs`
  - compiles the sidecar bridge
  - runs the ignored Rust command-handler smoke with `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR` unset
- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - includes `desktop-sidecar-discovery` in the objective-level entrypoint gate
- `package.json`
  - adds `verify:desktop-sidecar-discovery`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 441`
- `README.md`
  - lists `pnpm verify:desktop-sidecar-discovery` in Quick Start checks
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the 441 desktop sidecar default discovery smoke check
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 441 for handoff and resume continuity

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-desktop-sidecar-discovery-smoke.mjs`

## Iteration 439 - User Entrypoint Parity Gate

### Baseline

Iterations 430-438 proved individual surfaces: CLI output, skill wrapper
fallback, desktop compiled sidecar, Rust sidecar bridge, Tauri command
handlers, and mocked React UI invoke flow. The explicit user objective,
however, requires the product to work both through CLI skill invocation and
direct app use. A single objective-level gate should exercise all user
entrypoints and report one JSON evidence payload.

### Candidate Adopted

`user entrypoint parity gate`

Add a verifier that runs the existing user-facing smokes as one gate:
headless CLI, Codex Skill wrapper, desktop compiled sidecar, desktop Tauri
command handlers, and desktop React UI invoke flow.

### App Changes

- `scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
  - runs the existing headless CLI, Codex Skill wrapper, desktop sidecar,
    desktop Tauri command, and desktop React UI smokes
  - emits a single JSON summary mapping the objective to all user entrypoints
  - preserves fail-closed source cleanup semantics by checking the downstream
    smokes that keep `source/` present and require `DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT`
- `package.json`
  - adds `verify:entrypoints`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 439`
- `README.md`
  - lists `pnpm verify:entrypoints` in Quick Start checks
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the 439 user entrypoint parity gate check
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 439 for handoff and resume continuity

### Verification

- PASS: `node --check scripts/verify-entrypoints-smoke.mjs`
- PASS: `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`

- PASS: `pnpm typecheck`

- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `pnpm audit:brief` (`iterations: 439`, `allPassed: true`)
- no source snapshots have been pruned; the entrypoint gate relies on the
  underlying smokes that keep generated session `source/` snapshots present
  unless the explicit cleanup token is supplied

## Iteration 438 - Desktop Tauri Command Compiled Bridge Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 436 proved the React UI can consume mocked Tauri command payloads.
Iteration 435 proved Rust `call_sidecar` can drive the compiled Node sidecar.
The remaining app-direct gap is command mapping: `study_source`,
`list_sessions`, `resume_session`, `load_quiz`, `submit_quiz`, and
`source_prune_plan` must preserve the app payload fields when they call the
compiled bridge.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop tauri command compiled bridge smoke`

Add a direct Rust command-handler smoke so the app path is covered between the
mocked React invoke layer and the lower-level sidecar bridge helper.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - adds `REPOTUTOR_STUDIES_ROOT` passthrough for desktop command study/list
    sidecar calls so command smoke tests can use `/tmp` studies output
  - adds ignored Rust test `tauri_commands_drive_compiled_bridge`
  - directly exercises `study_source`, `list_sessions`, `resume_session`,
    `load_quiz`, `submit_quiz`, `source_prune_plan`, and the
    `apply_source_prune` confirmation-token guard
- `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar/bridge.ts`
  - normalizes `list` responses from full `StudySession` objects into desktop
    session rows with `mode`, `level`, `score`, `wrong`, `path`, and `html`
- `scripts/verify-desktop-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
  - now asserts the compiled sidecar list row contract before the Tauri
    command smoke consumes it
- `scripts/verify-desktop-tauri-commands-smoke.mjs`
  - builds runtime deps
  - compiles the Node sidecar bridge
  - runs the ignored Rust Tauri command mapping test with `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`
- `package.json`
  - adds `verify:desktop-tauri-commands`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 438`
- `README.md`
  - lists `pnpm verify:desktop-tauri-commands` in Quick Start checks
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the 438 desktop Tauri command compiled bridge smoke check
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 438 for handoff and resume continuity

## Iteration 437 - Codex Skill Wrapper Fallback Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 436 proved the desktop React UI can consume mock Tauri command
payloads directly. The remaining CLI/skill gap is distribution-level: the
installable skill wrapper must still run `study` through the shared CLI when a
machine has this project checkout but no global `repo-tutor` binary.

### Candidate Adopted

`codex skill wrapper fallback smoke`

Add a direct smoke for the Codex skill wrapper scripts so the skill invocation
path is tested as a user would run it from an installed skill: no global
`repo-tutor`, project root supplied through `REPOTUTOR_REPO_ROOT`, and the
shared CLI producing the same session artifacts.

### App Changes

- `scripts/verify-skill-wrapper-smoke.mjs`
  - creates a temporary PATH without `repo-tutor`

through `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`. The remaining app-direct gap is UI state: React
must render the Tauri command payloads into visible verification status,
source-retention status, quiz progress, submitted quiz result, and learning
record paths.

`desktop ui mock invoke smoke`

Add a direct React UI smoke that injects mock Tauri command payloads and proves
the desktop app screen can consume auto-resume, study, source-retention, quiz
load, and quiz submit payloads without relying only on command-level sidecar
checks.

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/tauri-api.ts`
  - wraps Tauri `invoke` and `convertFileSrc`
  - keeps production Tauri behavior unchanged
  - allows smoke tests to inject `window.__REPOTUTOR_STUDIO_TEST_API__`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - imports `invoke` and `convertFileSrc` from the wrapper
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.ui-smoke.tsx`
  - renders the React app in `happy-dom`
  - injects mock Tauri invoke responses for `list_sessions`, `resume_session`,
    `study_source`, `source_prune_plan`, `load_quiz`, and `submit_quiz`
  - verifies visible UI text for verification PASS, source retention,
    quiz progress, submitted score, and learning record path
- `package.json`
  - adds `happy-dom`
  - adds `verify:desktop-ui`

- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`

## Iteration 435 - Desktop Rust call_sidecar Compiled Bridge Smoke

### Baseline

Iteration 434 proved `node apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar-dist/sidecar/bridge.js`
directly handles study/list/resume/quiz/source-prune-plan after `tsconfig.node`
compilation. The remaining direct app boundary risk is Rust-side: `call_sidecar`
must spawn the same compiled bridge through `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`, write JSONL
stdin, parse the result envelope, and preserve the fields the Tauri commands
return to the desktop UI.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop rust call_sidecar compiled bridge smoke`

Add an ignored Rust test for the actual `call_sidecar` boundary and a root
verifier that compiles the Node sidecar, sets `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR`, and runs
that Rust test against the compiled bridge.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - adds ignored test `call_sidecar_drives_compiled_bridge`
  - requires `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR` to point at the compiled bridge
  - drives `study`, `list`, `resume`, `quiz`, and `sourcePrunePlan` through
    Rust `call_sidecar`
  - checks verification report paths, quiz artifacts, and dry-run prune output
- `scripts/verify-desktop-rust-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
  - builds runtime dependencies
  - compiles `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar/bridge.ts` to
    `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar-dist/sidecar/bridge.js`
  - runs the ignored Rust test with `REPOTUTOR_SIDECAR` set to that compiled
    bridge
- `package.json`
  - adds `verify:desktop-rust-sidecar`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 435`
- `README.md`
  - lists `pnpm verify:desktop-rust-sidecar` in Quick Start checks
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the 435 Rust `call_sidecar` compiled bridge smoke check
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 435 for handoff and resume continuity

  - drives `study`, `list`, `resume`, `quiz`, and `sourcePrunePlan` through

  - reports the compiled sidecar runner in the smoke summary

## Iteration 433 - Desktop Session Resume Parity

### Baseline

Iteration 432 made newly generated desktop `study` results expose the same
session verification handoff as CLI/Skill study output. A follow-up app parity
check found that selecting an existing session still constructed a partial
`StudyResponse` in the React app with only `html` and `quizQuestions: 0`, so
direct app users could lose the verification and learner-goal handoff after
reopening or selecting a prior session.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop session resume parity`

Make direct desktop app session selection use an explicit `resume` handoff,
matching the CLI `resume` contract for verification status, report paths,
learner-goal alignment, and quiz count.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar/bridge.ts`
  - adds a `resume` JSONL method
  - centralizes direct study/resume payload generation through
    `studyResponseFromSessionRoot`
  - reads `session.json` and `analysis/session-verification-report.json` to
    return verification fields and quiz counts for existing sessions
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - adds `resume_session`
  - routes through sidecar `resume` first, preserving CLI `repo-tutor resume
    --format json` fallback
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - makes session selection call `resume_session`
  - clears stale quiz state while switching sessions
  - logs the resumed session verification state
- `scripts/verify-desktop-sidecar-smoke.mjs`
  - checks sidecar `resume` returns the same session path, verification fields,
    report files, learner-goal alignment, and quiz count as the original study
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the 433 desktop session resume parity check
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 433`
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 433 for handoff and resume continuity

    fields matching the CLI quiz payload

  - makes `submit_quiz` call sidecar `quiz` first, preserving the existing CLI
    fallback when no sidecar is configured

retention panel wording now also names verification-record and current-goal

## Iteration 373 - Docs Source Cleanup Verification Record Boundary

### Baseline

Iteration 372 aligned prompt readiness with verification-record reporting
format. A follow-up scan found that generated app surfaces were aligned, but
operator-facing cleanup docs still summarized generated session `source/`
cleanup as preserved evidence plus current-goal confirmation and token. The
docs did not consistently name session verification and verification records.

External research cleanup also needed the same distinction: disposable
`research/external-src/` work copies can be pruned after a retained
no-further-analysis record, but that must never imply permission to prune a
learner's source, generated study-session `source/`, or product source.

### Candidate Adopted

`docs source cleanup verification record boundary`

README and the external-source lifecycle now require verification records
before cleanup decisions. The boundary keeps generated session cleanup separate
from disposable external research cache cleanup and keeps both separate from
user source and product source.

- `README.md`
  - requires session verification and verification records before generated
    session `source/` cleanup
  - updates Desktop retention control wording to verify preserved evidence,
    session verification, and verification records
- `docs/research/external-source-lifecycle.md`
  - requires retained verification records before pruning disposable
    `research/external-src/` work copies
  - requires a recorded no-further-analysis decision before external cache
    pruning
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - updates README and external-lifecycle checks and adds iteration 373 coverage
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 373`
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 373 for handoff and resume continuity

generated-output test suite cannot silently drift back to send-only wording.

prompt nearby carried the stronger final accept/deploy/delete boundary. Compact
cards and lead paragraphs are easy to skim without opening the full prompt, so

deploy, or delete. The labels remain only learner review candidates requiring
source evidence and verification checks.

recording what was absorbed and why no further analysis is needed for the
current research question before pruning, and separates external-cache cleanup
from generated study-session cleanup permission.

line when the session was apply-ready.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop retention decision prompt token boundary`

The copied decision prompt now always includes a fixed token-boundary line:
`DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT` is not permission created by `READY_REVIEW`; it is the
learner's final current-goal confirmation that source links no longer need to
open.

## Iteration 335 - Source Prune Explicit Confirmation Guard Boundary

### Baseline

Iteration 334 aligned the CLI source-prune preserved evidence bundle with core
and desktop. The next scan checked whether every source-prune review surface
keeps `READY_REVIEW` framed as a review candidate instead of cleanup
permission. Core dry-run Markdown and the desktop prompt preview already said
`READY_REVIEW` is not deletion permission. The CLI `CLI 정리 판단` summary,
however, only said the generated session `source/` snapshot was an apply
candidate after learner confirmation. A learner reading only the terminal
summary could miss the explicit `READY_REVIEW` boundary. The destructive core
API also accepted direct `applySourcePrunePlan(sessionRoot)` calls, while CLI
and desktop wrappers enforced the `DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT` token.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune explicit confirmation guard boundary`

The source-prune apply guard now requires the explicit
`DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT` token at the core API boundary, and both CLI and the
desktop sidecar pass that token through after their existing prompt checks. The
CLI cleanup decision summary now states that `READY_REVIEW` is a cleanup review
candidate, not deletion permission. The post-apply tombstone records that the
core guard requires the token and that UI/CLI prompts should collect it only
after the learner checks the dry-run plan, preserved evidence bundle, and
current-goal source-link confirmation.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - adds `SOURCE_PRUNE_CONFIRMATION_TOKEN`
  - requires `{ confirm: "DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT" }` before deleting the
    generated session `source/` snapshot
  - records the explicit confirmation boundary in `SOURCE-PRUNED.md`
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - passes the confirmation token into `applySourcePrunePlan`
  - adds a `READY_REVIEW 경계` line to the CLI cleanup decision summary
- `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar/bridge.ts`
  - passes the existing desktop confirmation token into the core apply guard
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies direct core apply without confirmation is rejected
  - verifies the confirmed apply path still writes the tombstone and retained
    learning records
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds iteration 335 coverage for the explicit confirmation guard boundary
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to `--iterations 335`
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 335 as the latest resume point

## Iteration 331 - Wrong Notes Selected Rationale Source Path Channel Formatting

### Baseline

Iteration 330 made the static quiz pages show wrong-choice rationales, including
the source-retention warning that generated session `source/` snapshots are
temporary evidence rather than app knowledge. The follow-up scan checked the
post-attempt learning loop. When a learner actually chose the source-retention
wrong answer, `wrong-notes.md`, `wrong-notes.html`, and the quiz learning record
kept the generic review prompt but lost the selected choice rationale. That
made the durable remediation weaker than the pre-attempt quiz page.

`wrong notes selected rationale source path channel formatting`

Wrong notes now preserve the rationale for the learner's selected wrong choice.
For the source-retention misconception, JSON stores the raw plain text, Markdown
outputs use `` `source/` ``, and HTML wrong-note cards use
`<code>source/</code>`. Quiz-answer and CLI prompt channels remain plain.

  - includes selected-choice remediation in quiz attempt learning records
  - keeps the learning-record channel in Markdown backticks

## Iteration 330 - Quiz HTML Source Path Body Formatting

### Baseline

Iteration 329 left quiz-answer and CLI channels as plain text, but the next
scan found a split inside the static quiz export. The generated quiz JSON
already explains that the user's original source is not app knowledge and that
the generated session `source/` snapshot is temporary project evidence. However,
`quiz.html` and `quiz-print.html` rendered only the correct answer explanation,
so the source-retention wrong-choice rationale was not visible in the rendered
HTML learner surface. That also meant the HTML body channel had no test proving
that this `source/` wording is formatted as a code path.

### Candidate Adopted

`quiz html source path body formatting`

The quiz HTML renderer now treats visible quiz body text as an HTML body
channel. Choice labels, questions, explanations, and the newly rendered
wrong-choice rationales pass through `quizBodyTextHtml`, which turns generated
session `source/` references into `<code>source/</code>`. The JSON analysis file
and interactive CLI prompts remain plain text, preserving the channel boundary.

  - asserts that `analysis/quiz.json` keeps the same wording as plain

## Iteration 328 - HTML Inline Code Path Markdown-Origin Normalization

### Baseline

After iteration 327, the desktop React renderer could normalize Markdown-origin
`` `source/` `` before displaying visible body text. A parallel edge remained
in the static HTML template layer: `inlineCodePathHtml` and the body sections
that call it could receive strings that were already Markdown-formatted, while
some detection logic still looked only for plain `source/`.

### Candidate Adopted

`html inline code path markdown-origin normalization`

The static HTML inline code-path renderer now normalizes backticked code-path
tokens before escaping and wrapping them in `<code>`. The vibe-start next
action list, teaching workspace card descriptions, and improvement backlog
source-retention action all use the same normalization helper before deciding
whether a generated session `source/` path belongs in an HTML body code tag.

    source-retention body text before detection

should stay plain, while visible React body strings already route through
`sourceSnapshotCodePathNode`. One weak edge remained: body strings that arrive
from Markdown-origin data can contain backticked `` `source/` `` and the
desktop renderer would split on `source/` while leaving the surrounding
backticks visible.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source path renderer markdown-origin normalization`

The desktop source-path renderer now normalizes Markdown-origin `` `source/` ``
to plain `source/` before rendering the visible body text as
`<code>source/</code>`. This preserves plain prompt/title/clipboard channels
while making the body renderer robust for source-prune and retained-learning
data that may already be Markdown-formatted upstream.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - normalizes `` `source/` `` to `source/` inside `sourceSnapshotCodePathNode`
  - splits rendered body text on the normalized value so React output shows a
    clean `<code>source/</code>` path without leaked Markdown backticks
  - leaves text with no source path untouched
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - updates the older desktop body code-path audit to the normalized renderer
  - adds iteration 327 coverage for Markdown-origin source-path normalization
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 327
- `working.md`
  - records iteration 327 as the latest completed slice with verification
    evidence and the next resume point

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS

## Iteration 280 - Source Prune Generated Session Source Cleanup Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 279, product docs separated generated study-session `source/`
snapshots from the learner's original repository or local folder. The generated
source prune plan and SOURCE-PRUNED tombstone still used learner-facing
`원본 source/` wording, which could be read as if RepoTutor was pruning or
retaining the learner's original source rather than the generated session
snapshot.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune generated session source cleanup wording`

The prune plan and tombstone now call the cleanup target the generated session
`source/` snapshot. Re-analysis guidance says to create a fresh session
snapshot from the same original repo or folder if retained artifacts are
insufficient, while explicitly avoiding long-term app knowledge storage of the
user's original source.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - changes prune-plan learner guidance from `원본 source/` to generated
    session `source/` snapshot wording
  - changes SOURCE-PRUNED tombstone learner guidance to clarify that only the
    generated session snapshot was temporary evidence
  - changes re-analysis guidance to recreate a new session snapshot from the
    same original repo/folder when retained artifacts are insufficient
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the new generated-session wording in the prune plan and tombstone
  - keeps negative assertions against the older `원본 source/` runtime wording
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds iteration 280 coverage for source-prune generated-session cleanup
    wording
  - forbids the old runtime `원본 source/` prune/tombstone wording
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 280

## Iteration 279 - Learning Mission Generated Session Source Cleanup Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 278, the storage model clearly separated the learner's original
source, the generated study-session `source/` copy, and disposable external
research work copies. The learning mission still ended with broad wording that
the `cloned source should be deleted`, which could be read as a general source
deletion policy instead of a narrow disposable-work-copy cleanup rule.

### Candidate Adopted

`learning mission generated session source cleanup wording`

The learning mission now states that generated study-session `source/`
snapshots are evidence copies, not the learner's original repository or local
folder. Cleanup may target only the generated session snapshot after retained
learning artifacts, verification records, and explicit learner confirmation
exist for the current learning goal. External repository examples are described
as disposable external-source work copies that can be pruned and re-cloned only
when fresh evidence is needed.

- `docs/product/learning-mission.md`
  - adds the generated study-session `source/` snapshot boundary

  - changes broad cloned-source deletion wording to disposable external-source
    work-copy pruning

## Iteration 278 - Storage Model Generated Session Source Cleanup Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 277, external-source research cleanup was scoped to disposable
work copies. The product storage model still used broad terms such as `source
snapshot cleanup`, `User-provided source snapshots`, `original source/ folder`,
and `Temporary research clones`, which could blur the difference between the
learner's original source, the generated study-session `source/` copy, and
disposable external research work copies.

### Candidate Adopted

`storage model generated session source cleanup wording`

The storage model now describes the study folder's `source/` as a generated
per-session snapshot copied from user-provided source. Cleanup is scoped to that
generated session snapshot, explicitly excludes the learner's original repo or
local folder, and aligns external research cleanup with disposable work-copy
wording.

- `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - clarifies that the durable study folder contains a generated `source/`
    snapshot, not a permanent source-code library
  - changes cleanup policy text from user-provided/original source wording to
    generated session `source/` snapshot wording
  - clarifies tombstone-aware verification for pruned generated session
    snapshots
  - aligns `research/external-src/` language with disposable external-source
    work copies

  - adds iteration 278 coverage for storage-model generated-session cleanup

  - forbids the old broad storage-model source cleanup phrases

## Iteration 270 - Source Cleanup Review Wording

teach the wrong model: source cleanup is not broad deletion, and the learner is
reviewing whether only the generated session `source/` snapshot can be cleaned
after durable learning evidence remains.

### Candidate Adopted

`source cleanup review wording`

Desktop source-purpose copy, mastery checklist prompts, and the applied prune
tombstone now use `정리 검토` / `정리 후` wording instead of `삭제 가능` /
`삭제 후` wording.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - changes the source cleanup checkpoint from `삭제 가능 조건` to
    `정리 검토 조건`
  - changes the source purpose contract to say generated session `source/`
    snapshots are judged for cleanup review, not deletion permission
  - changes the retention decision prompt so even a review candidate only
    targets the generated session `source/` snapshot
- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - changes the mastery checklist source-use prompt to ask whether generated
    `source/` snapshot cleanup can be reviewed
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - changes the `SOURCE-PRUNED.md` learner asset note from `삭제 후 학습` to
    `정리 후 학습`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - adds generated output assertions and negative assertions for the old
    deletion-framed strings
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds and tightens gates for the new cleanup review wording
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 270

## Iteration 269 - Teaching Workspace Source Retention Cleanup Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 268, the desktop source retention panel used generated-snapshot
cleanup wording. Generated learning surfaces still had labels such as

`삭제를 검토합니다`. Those phrases could still imply broad source deletion rather
than reviewed cleanup of generated session `source/` snapshots after retained
evidence exists.

## Iteration 268 - Desktop Source Retention Pruned Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 267, the desktop quiz used answer-state wording. The desktop
source retention panel still used deletion/completion-framed copy when a
generated session `source/` snapshot had been pruned: `삭제 완료`,
`소스 스냅샷 정리 완료`, and `원본 source/ 스냅샷은 삭제됐고`. Those phrases could
make the action sound like broad source deletion instead of reviewed cleanup of
only the generated session snapshot.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source retention pruned wording`

The desktop source retention panel now frames pruned state as reviewed cleanup
of the generated session source snapshot, not as a general source deletion.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - changes the pruned source display from `삭제 완료` to `정리됨`
  - changes the apply log from `소스 스냅샷 정리 완료` to
    `생성된 source/ 스냅샷 정리 적용됨`
  - changes the pruned recommendation from `원본 source/ 스냅샷은 삭제됐고`
    to `생성된 세션 source/ 스냅샷은 정리됐고`
  - changes the prompt title and preview copy from `보존/삭제 판단` to
    `보존/정리 판단`
  - keeps negative evidence assertions `not.toContain("삭제 완료")` and
    `not.toContain("소스 스냅샷 정리 완료")` for the desktop app source
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`

  - adds the `desktop source retention pruned wording` gate
  - raises the relevant gate to `--iterations 268`
- `package.json`

## Iteration 267 - Desktop Quiz Unanswered Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 266, quiz learning records no longer used completion framing.
The desktop quiz workspace still labelled unanswered questions as `미완료 문항`.
Those labels were operationally correct, but the actual state is that the
learner has not selected an answer yet. `미응답 문항` is more precise and avoids
implying that the learner's job is to complete a traditional course checklist.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop quiz unanswered wording`

The desktop quiz workspace now uses `미응답 문항` wording for summary, shortcuts,
filters, navigation logs, and empty-state copy.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - changes quiz missing-question summary from `미완료 문항` to `미응답 문항`
  - changes missing-only filter, shortcut aria labels, navigation logs, and
    empty-state copy to use `미응답`
  - keeps the negative evidence assertion `not.toContain("미완료 문항")` for the
    desktop app source
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - updates the existing desktop quiz gates to require the new wording
  - adds the `desktop quiz unanswered wording` gate
  - raises the relevant gate to `--iterations 267`
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 267

## Iteration 266 - Quiz Learning Record Non-Completion Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 265, AI output review wording no longer treated completion as
the main frame. Quiz learning records still said `이 기록은 이해 완료가 아니라`
for review attempts and `문법 암기 완료표가 아니라` in the vibe-coding evidence
section. Both lines were intended as warnings, but the word `완료` still made
the record sound like a completion certificate rather than a source-grounded
learning signal for AI prompting and review.

`quiz learning record non-completion wording`

Quiz learning records now say `이 기록은 이해 확정 선언이 아니라` and
`문법 암기표가 아니라 AI에게 줄 구현 맥락 준비도입니다`.

## Iteration 265 - AI Output Review Block Acceptance Verification Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 264, generated rebuild guidance required verification before
asking AI to act or accepting AI output. The AI Output Review Rubric still had
a BLOCK row that said `테스트나 실행 없이 완료를 단정하거나 실패 시 보고 형식이
없습니다`. That correctly blocks weak AI output, but it still centers the idea
of completion. For a vibe-coding learner, the sharper lesson is that AI output
must not be accepted without verification records.

### Candidate Adopted

`ai output review block acceptance verification wording`

The AI Output Review Rubric BLOCK wording now says
`검증 기록 없이 수락을 요구하거나 실패 시 보고 형식이 없습니다`.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - changes the AI Output Review Rubric verification-boundary BLOCK row from a
    completion assertion to an unverified acceptance risk
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated AI Output Review Rubric HTML and Markdown contain
    `검증 기록 없이 수락을 요구하거나 실패 시 보고 형식이 없습니다`
  - asserts the old `테스트나 실행 없이 완료를 단정하거나 실패 시 보고 형식이 없습니다`
    wording is absent
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the `ai output review block acceptance verification wording` gate
  - raises the relevant gate to `--iterations 265`
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 265

## Iteration 264 - Rebuild Cheatsheet Acceptance Verification Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 263, generated prompt and A/B Lab surfaces increasingly framed
AI output as something the learner accepts only after review. The Rebuild
Cheatsheet still used a generic `Completion Criteria` heading and said each
slice should define verification `완료 전에`. That wording could still make a
vibe-coding learner think the central skill is proving work is finished, rather
than defining checks before asking AI to act and before accepting AI output.

### Candidate Adopted

`rebuild cheatsheet acceptance verification wording`

The Rebuild Cheatsheet now uses `수락/검증 기준` and says each slice must define
verification before sending an AI request or accepting the result.

    request is sent or the result is accepted

    `<h3>수락/검증 기준</h3>`
  - asserts the old `각 slice는 완료 전에` and

## Iteration 263 - Prompt A/B Lab Success Claim Verification Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 262, the A/B Lab's acceptance criteria weakness was framed as
`수락/검증 기준`. Another weak Prompt A row still said
`AI가 성공이라고 말하면 끝나는 구조입니다`. That line correctly marks the prompt
as weak, but "ends when AI says success" can still normalize completion as the
main frame. The product goal is to teach learners to require verification
records before accepting AI output.

### Candidate Adopted

`prompt ab lab success claim verification wording`

The weak Prompt A explanation now says
`AI가 성공이라고 말하면 검증 기록 없이 수락하는 구조입니다`.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - changes the A/B Lab failure-reporting weakness from a stopping-point frame
    to an unverified acceptance risk

## Iteration 262 - Prompt A/B Lab Acceptance Verification Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 261, learner-facing acceptance criteria wording was reframed as
`수락/검증 기준`. The generated AI Prompt A/B Lab still described a weak Prompt
A with `완료 여부가 흐립니다`. That wording can pull the learner back toward
asking whether AI is done instead of whether the prompt has observable criteria
for accepting or revising the output.

`prompt ab lab acceptance verification wording`

The weak Prompt A explanation now says
`잘 동작하게 해달라는 말만 있어 수락/검증 기준이 흐립니다`.

  - asserts generated A/B Lab HTML and Markdown contain

  - asserts the old `완료 여부가 흐립니다` wording is absent

## Iteration 261 - Learner Acceptance Verification Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 260, generated prompt sequence wording treated AI output as a
current artifact to review. A few learner-facing surfaces still used completion
phrases such as `완료 기준`, `완료 검증 질문`, `AI가 끝났다고 주장할 기준`, and
`기능이 끝났다고 판단할 수 있는 관찰 가능한 조건입니다`. Those phrases can
make a vibe-coding learner think the goal is to prove AI is done, rather than
define observable criteria for reviewing whether the AI output should be
accepted, revised, or blocked.

### Candidate Adopted

`learner acceptance verification wording`

The generated learner surfaces now keep acceptance criteria as a useful concept
but frame it as `수락/검증 기준`, `수락/검증 질문`, and observable learner
review criteria.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - changes learner role prompts from `완료 기준` to `수락/검증 기준`
  - changes AI output review wording from completion-context repair to
    `맥락이나 수락/검증 기준을 보강해야 합니다`
  - changes similar app transfer verification from `AI가 끝났다고 주장할 기준`
    to `학습자가 수락/검증할 관찰 기준`
  - changes goal alignment signals from `완료/수락 기준` to
    `수락/검증 기준`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - changes folder vertical slice prompts from `완료 검증 질문` to
    `수락/검증 질문`
  - changes PRD and acceptance criteria glossary definitions to
    `수락/검증 기준` and `기능을 수락할 수 있는 관찰 가능한 조건입니다`
  - changes an expected mistake from trusting completion to accepting AI output
    without TDD or acceptance criteria
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated JSON, HTML, and Markdown contain the new wording
  - asserts the old completion framing is absent from those generated surfaces
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the `learner acceptance verification wording` gate
  - raises the relevant gate to `--iterations 261`
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 261

  tests, while old completion wording appears only in audit forbidden strings
  and test negative assertions
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS

## Iteration 260 - Prompt Sequence Current Artifact Review Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 259, the generated prompt-pack verification rubric framed AI
output as a change to verify. Two generated prompt sequence lines still used
finality wording: `최종 결과를 학습자 문서로 바꿔줘`, `끝났다고 가정하고
리뷰해줘`, and the fallback `최종 결과를 리뷰해줘`. Those lines can make the
learner treat AI output as a final answer instead of a current artifact that
needs review.

### Candidate Adopted

`prompt sequence current artifact review wording`

The learning-journal document prompt now says
`현재 산출물을 학습자 문서 초안으로 바꿔줘`, and the prompt-pack review step now
says `구현됐다고 가정하고 리뷰해줘` or `현재 산출물을 리뷰해줘`.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - changes the learning-journal document prompt from final result to current
    artifact draft
  - changes the vibe prompt pack review prompt from final result/completion
    framing to current implementation/artifact review
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated learning journal JSON, HTML, and Markdown contain the new
    current-artifact document prompt
  - asserts generated vibe prompt pack JSON, HTML, and Markdown contain
    `구현됐다고 가정하고 리뷰해줘`
  - asserts the old final-result review strings are absent
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the `prompt sequence current artifact review wording` gate
  - raises the relevant gate to `--iterations 260`
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 260

## Iteration 259 - Prompt Pack Rubric Verification Result Wording

After iteration 258, the generated implementation prompt asked AI for
`변경 뒤 확인할 명령` rather than `완료 후 확인할 명령`. The agent instruction
skeleton inside the generated Vibe-Coding Prompt Pack still said
`어떤 명령, 테스트, 화면, 리포트로 완료를 확인할지 먼저 적는다`. That line
could still frame the learner's job as confirming completion rather than
reviewing a change with evidence.

`prompt pack rubric verification result wording`

The verification rubric now says
`어떤 명령, 테스트, 화면, 리포트로 변경 결과를 검증할지 먼저 적는다`.

## Iteration 256 - Shared AI Output Review Target Candidate Wording

framed AI output review as `PASS_REVIEW/REVISE/BLOCK` review status rather than

## Iteration 255 - Teaching Workspace Learning Evidence Record Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 254, the HTML report index still described
`learning-records` with `학습 완료 기록은 퀴즈 시도처럼 증거가 생긴 뒤에만
생성됩니다`. That wording was better than unconditional completion, but it
still framed records as completion artifacts. For this app, records should be
evidence that a vibe-coding learner can resume, brief AI, and review AI output;
they should not imply traditional lesson completion or coding mastery.

`teaching workspace learning evidence record wording`

The Teaching Workspace index card now says
`학습 증거 기록은 퀴즈 시도처럼 근거가 생긴 뒤에만 생성됩니다`.

    product's source-grounded evidence language

  - regenerated by `pnpm --filter @repotutor/html build` so runtime package

## Iteration 254 - CLI Source Prune Current-Goal Investigation Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 253, the source absorption ledger avoided absolute
investigation-closure wording. The CLI `prune-source` cleanup decision still
said `조사 종료 확인`. In a cleanup flow, that can sound like all analysis is
finished, even though the app only supports evidence-preserving cleanup after
the current learning goal, retained artifacts, source retention guide, and
learner confirmation all line up.

### Candidate Adopted

`cli source prune current-goal investigation wording`

The CLI cleanup decision now says `현재 목표 조사 확인`, and the line explains
that `reference/source-retention-guide.html` and `markdown/source-prune-plan.md`
must describe the investigation needs that remain for the current learning goal.

### App Changes

- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - changes the source cleanup decision line from `조사 종료 확인` to
    `현재 목표 조사 확인`
  - scopes the check to `현재 학습 목표에서 남은 조사 필요 여부`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - strengthens the `cli source prune cleanup decision` gate with the new
    current-goal wording and a forbidden check for the old CLI phrase
  - raises the relevant CLI source prune gate to `--iterations 254`
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 254

### Same-Sample Verification

## Iteration 253 - Source Absorption Current-Goal Summary Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 252, `source-absorption-ledger` already documented which app
features and learning artifacts RepoTutor absorbed, and it scoped
`noFurtherResearch` rows with `현재 학습 목표 기준:`. However, the summary
section still used `조사 종료 요약`, and the AI confirmation prompt said
`PASS_REVIEW도 조사 종료, source 삭제, 최종 승인 허가가 아니라`. Those were
guarded phrases, but they could still sound like whole-investigation closure
instead of current-goal review status.

### Candidate Adopted

`source absorption current-goal summary wording`

The source absorption ledger now uses `현재 목표 조사 요약`, and the AI
confirmation prompt now says `PASS_REVIEW도 현재 항목 검토 후보일 뿐 전체 조사 종료,
source 삭제, 최종 승인 허가가 아니라`.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - changes the source absorption summary heading from `조사 종료 요약` to
    `현재 목표 조사 요약`
  - changes the AI confirmation prompt to frame `PASS_REVIEW` as a current-item
    review candidate, not whole-investigation closure
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`

  - adds negative assertions for the older direct user-facing phrases
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`

    gates with the new current-goal wording and forbidden checks for the old
    phrases
  - raises the relevant source absorption gates to `--iterations 253`
- `package.json`

## Iteration 233 - Desktop Review Checklist Toggle Log Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 232, two desktop checklist toggle logs still used `완료` for a
single checked criterion:

- `AI 응답 검토 완료: ...`
- `AI 구현 결과 검토 완료: ...`

In the clarified product model, one checked criterion is not the same as a
completed review. The log should describe the item state without implying that
the whole AI response or implementation-result review is complete.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop review checklist toggle log wording`

Both review checklist toggle handlers now use `체크됨` for a newly checked
criterion and keep `해제` for an unchecked criterion.

## Iteration 231 - Scanner Static Caveat Verification Wording

### Baseline

After iteration 230, static scanner learner-next-step caveats still used
final-verdict wording: `실제 취약점 판정`, `실제 버그 판정`, `Pact 도구를 실행해
판정`, and `실제 drift 판정`. These reports are static readiness surfaces; they
should teach the learner to verify in the original project, not treat RepoTutor
as the final judge.

### Candidate Adopted

`scanner static caveat verification wording`

The VEX, Schemathesis/API contract, Pact consumer contract, and OpenAPI client
readiness caveats now use verification wording:

- `statementDrafts는 실제 취약점 확인 결과가 아니라 pending triage 템플릿입니다.`
- `실제 버그 여부는 원본 API에서 별도 실행해 확인하세요.`
- `실제 contract pass/fail은 원본 프로젝트에서 Pact 도구를 실행해 확인하세요.`
- `실제 drift 여부는 원본 저장소에서 validate/generate/typecheck를 별도 실행해 확인하세요.`

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - changes four learner-next-step caveats from final `판정` wording to
    original-project verification wording
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated JSON, HTML, and Markdown artifacts contain the new VEX,
    API contract, Pact, and OpenAPI client caveats
  - rejects the old `판정` caveat wording in those generated artifacts
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the `scanner static caveat verification wording` gate
  - raises the gate requirement to `--iterations 231`
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 231

decision rather than a review-state candidate.

ready to review the next AI request candidate. The affected prompts and start
surfaces explicitly say `READY_REVIEW` is a learner review state, not permission
to send a prompt, delete source snapshots, or accept AI output.

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`

    `READY_REVIEW`
  - updates self-evaluation prompts to request `READY_REVIEW / REVIEW / BLOCKED`
    and `READY_REVIEW / REVISE / BLOCK`
  - adds warnings that `READY_REVIEW` is not send, delete, or ACCEPT permission
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - updates `vibe-coding-start.md` guidance to describe `READY_REVIEW` as a
    pre-send learner review state
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - updates generated start-page cards and next-action text to the same
    `READY_REVIEW` review-state wording
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - updates representative generated-session assertions and adds negative
    assertions for the old `READY` permission-sounding labels
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds `generated readiness labels review-state gate` with forbidden coverage
    for old READY phrasing

## Iteration 196 - Learning Path Evidence Review Marker Wording

### Baseline

Iteration 195 tightened desktop clipboard and prompt-copy wording. The next
generated artifact scan found `learning-path.html` progress labels that still
used completion-sounding copy: "근거 확인 완료 0 /", "근거 확인 후 완료", and
runtime JS text `근거 확인 완료`. In the app's clarified model, checking a
learning-path box should mark source evidence review, not prove that the learner
has finished learning or can skip later verification.

Generated learning-path progress now says "근거 검토 표시" and the checkbox
label says "근거 확인 후 검토 표시". The localStorage-backed browser progress
counter uses the same wording so saved progress remains a review marker rather
than a completion claim.

## Iteration 195 - Desktop Prompt Clipboard Pre-Send Review Gate

### Baseline

Iteration 194 scoped cleanup apply commands to the current learning goal. A
follow-up desktop prompt-copy scan found readiness, clipboard, and next-action
surfaces that could still sound executable before learner review. Examples
included "AI 지시 가능", "준비 완료", "AI 응답 검토가 완료되었습니다",
"다음 작은 개선으로 이동", generic clipboard-save wording, and ACCEPT-related
labels that could imply final acceptance before the learner checks evidence.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop prompt clipboard pre-send review gate`

Desktop prompt-copy surfaces now frame copied prompts, READY/ACCEPT language,
and next actions as pre-send review candidates. The learner must check goals,
source evidence, acceptance criteria, verification criteria, changed files,
failures/risks, and direct confirmation before sending an AI prompt or treating
AI output as accepted.

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - changes learner-brief readiness from "AI 지시 가능" to "AI 지시 초안 검토 가능"

    clipboard log, button tooltip, and hero wording so they all require
    pre-send learner review instead of implying immediate execution

  - adds/updates compliance checks for prompt clipboard review wording,
    learner-brief readiness, AI response revision prompts, implementation
    decision summaries, handoff prompts, and old executable-sounding forbidden
    strings

  - raises `audit:brief` to iteration 195

candidates until the learner confirms purpose, evidence, acceptance criteria,
and verification records.

## Iteration 174 - Prompt A/B Lab Send Decision Candidate Wording

### Baseline

The generated AI prompt A/B lab still said the learner should send Prompt B
after checking its source evidence and should send a revised prompt after
changing it. That was too direct for the user's review-first model, where even
the better prompt is a candidate until the learner confirms product fit,
evidence, acceptance criteria, and verification assertions.

`prompt ab lab send decision candidate wording`

sends REVISE cases back through another review pass. HTML fixture tests assert
the new wording and reject the older direct send phrasing.

## Iteration 172 - Teaching Workspace Mastery Ready Candidate Wording

### Baseline

The generated vibe-coding mastery checklist still used direct readiness wording
for two READY rows: the learner could request an implementation prompt with
purpose/input/output/completion criteria, and could judge AI output as
PASS/REVISE/BLOCK after separating static and runtime evidence. That phrasing
blurred the user's intended model: READY should mean the learner can create and
review a candidate, not automatically send or accept it.

### Candidate Adopted

`teaching workspace mastery ready candidate wording`

The mastery checklist now describes the first implementation prompt as a
request candidate to review and adapt, and describes PASS/REVISE/BLOCK as a
judgment candidate based on separated evidence. HTML and Markdown fixture tests
assert the new wording and reject the older direct request/judgment phrasing.

send-ready instructions.

## Iteration 170 - Desktop AI Response Next-Step Execution Guard Wording

### Baseline

The desktop AI-response revision prompt said that, after all review checks were
complete, the next step was to execute the proposed first vertical slice and
compare acceptance criteria with test results. That was still too execution
forward for the clarified product model: the learner should first turn AI output
into reviewed implementation candidates, acceptance criteria, and a verification
comparison plan.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop AI response next-step execution guard wording`

The completed-review branch now tells the learner to review the proposed first
vertical slice, split it into small implementation candidates, and confirm
acceptance criteria plus the test-comparison method before execution. The audit
gate forbids the older direct execution wording from returning.

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - changes the AI-response revision prompt's completed-review next step from
    direct execution to review-first implementation candidate preparation
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`

    iterations

## Question

RepoTutor should help a vibe-coding learner study a GitHub repository or source
folder without turning raw source into permanent product knowledge. The weak
point was not analysis coverage; it was that each session lacked a focused
reference page that tells the learner what to preserve before deleting or
pruning a copied source snapshot.

## Baseline

- Product docs already said source is evidence, not app-wide product knowledge.
- `daily-summary-report.json` already carried `sourceHandling` and
  `cleanupGuidance`.
- `source-snapshot-report.json` already recorded file digests, sizes, and
  tracked status.
- The teaching workspace did not expose a dedicated retention guide under
  `reference/`, so the learner had to infer cleanup rules from broader pages.

## Candidate

Add `reference/source-retention-guide.html` to every teaching workspace.

The guide is generated from existing session evidence and includes:

- source snapshot file count, total byte size, and creation time
- deletion-before-checking guardrails
- retained learning artifacts from `dailySummaryReport.sourceHandling`
- cleanup principles
- large file candidates for capacity review
- a copyable AI prompt that keeps the learner's role clear:
  "나는 전문 개발자가 아니라 바이브코딩 개발자야..."

## Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/source-retention-guide.html` to
    `TEACHING_WORKSPACE_REQUIRED_ARTIFACTS`
  - writes the new reference file during `writeTeachingWorkspaceArtifacts`
  - links it from `RESOURCES.md`
  - records the retention checkpoint in `NOTES.md`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies the artifact exists in a full study session
  - checks the key learner-facing Korean strings
  - checks `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md` point to the guide
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - requires the new retention guide strings in the stateful teaching workspace
    compliance check

## Same-Sample Verification

- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "creates teaching workspace state"`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "generates a complete study session"`: PASS
- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `allPassed: true`
- `pnpm build`: PASS
- CLI smoke:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- study packages/core/tests/fixtures/simple-ts-app --mode quick --level beginner --format json --studies-root /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-retention-smoke --no-codex`
  - PASS
  - `verificationOk: true`
  - `verificationCheckedRequiredArtifacts: 728`
  - `reference/source-retention-guide.html` contains the retention guide title,
    source-as-evidence language, current source snapshot section, deletion
    checkpoint, and AI confirmation prompt

## Not Adopted

- No automatic source deletion was added. User-provided session snapshots remain
  evidence and should be removed only by explicit user action or a future
  retention control.
- No external repository was cloned or embedded for this slice.
- No external source cache was created.

## Cleanup Status

There is no `research/external-src/` cache to delete for this slice. The durable
record is this note plus the implemented artifact, tests, and compliance check.

## Iteration 121 - Quiz Prompt Review Structured Fields

### Observation

Iteration 120 moved prompt/review guidance into `analysis/glossary.json`, but
quiz generation still used a generic `answer` string for every topic. That kept
quiz data closer to definition checking than the user's target: a vibe-coding
learner should practice turning source and term evidence into AI instructions,
AI output review questions, and "do not memorize this" boundaries.

### Candidate Adopted

`quiz prompt review structured fields`

The candidate adds `aiPromptUse`, `aiReviewQuestion`, and
`memorizationWarning` to quiz questions. Folder, file, and glossary quiz topics
now carry an AI instruction sentence, an AI output review question, and a
memorization warning. Quiz choices, explanations, and wrong-note review text
use these fields so quiz artifacts reinforce prompt/review judgment instead of
traditional syntax memorization.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. This slice only changes
generated quiz learning artifacts and verification metadata.

## Iteration 122 - Teaching Glossary Prompt Review Reference

### Observation

The main glossary and quiz artifacts now carry prompt/review guidance, but the
stateful teaching workspace also has a separate `reference/glossary.html`.
That reference already warned against syntax memorization, yet each term card
still looked closer to a definition sheet: definition, project meaning, and a
single source example. A learner resuming from the workspace could miss the
same "turn this term into an AI instruction and AI output review question"
contract.

### Candidate Adopted

`teaching glossary prompt review reference`

The candidate updates `reference/glossary.html` cards with
`data-glossary-use="ai-prompt-review-reference"`, `Source evidence:`,
`AI에게 지시할 문장`, `AI 출력 리뷰 질문`, and `외우지 말 것`. The card content uses
`term.promptUse`, `term.reviewQuestion`, and `term.memorizationWarning`, so the
resumable teaching workspace matches the main glossary and quiz prompt/review
model.

## Iteration 123 - Source Absorption AI Confirmation Prompts

### Observation

The source absorption ledger already recorded what the app absorbed and whether
additional investigation was needed, but each row still stopped at a human
description. For the user's workflow, this should become a copy-ready AI review
prompt: the learner needs to ask AI whether the preserved artifact is enough,
whether the "no further research" judgment is valid, and what to do if it is
weak.

### Candidate Adopted

`source absorption ai confirmation prompts`

The candidate adds `sourceAbsorptionAiConfirmationPrompt` and renders
`AI에게 줄 확인 프롬프트` for every absorbed row in
`reference/source-absorption-ledger.html` and `.md`. The prompt asks AI to check
the preserved artifact, validate `추가 조사 판단`, answer
`PASS / REVISE / BLOCK`, and provide `부족하면 추가 조사 질문 3개` plus a next
prompt when the evidence is weak.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. This slice only strengthens
the source absorption decision artifact.

## Iteration 126 - Teaching Workspace Rebuild Prompt Guide Card

### Observation

The portable Teaching Workspace `reference/rebuild` card still described the
rebuild reference as a quick copy/paste prompt shortcut. That was weaker than
the product direction and weaker than current AI-coding guidance: source
evidence should become scoped context, acceptance criteria, tests, and review
criteria, not just a prompt to paste.

### External Evidence

GitHub Copilot official prompt guidance supports this change:

- GitHub Copilot official best practices say to provide helpful context and
  specific repositories, files, symbols, or task focus where relevant:
  https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/get-started/best-practices
- GitHub Copilot official testing guidance says useful context, such as nearby
  test files and the testing framework, helps generated tests fit the existing
  project:
  https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/tutorials/write-tests
- GitHub Copilot official rollout guidance warns against letting AI guess
  undocumented business rules and against treating AI as a substitute for human
  code review:
  https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/tutorials/roll-out-at-scale/drive-downstream-impact/increase-test-coverage

### Candidate Adopted

`teaching workspace rebuild prompt guide card`

The existing `reference/rebuild-cheatsheet.html` artifact path remains for
compatibility, but the Teaching Workspace card copy now says the reference
turns the first vertical slice, acceptance criteria, and verification plan into
an AI implementation prompt guide.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. This slice only changes
portable Teaching Workspace copy and verification coverage.

## Iteration 130 - Daily Summary Prompt Reuse Source-Grounded Rewrite

### External Basis

GitHub Copilot official guidance says thoughtful prompts should break down
complex tasks, be specific about requirements, provide examples/context, and
then evaluate Copilot's response. The daily summary should therefore push
learners to adapt prompts with source evidence and verification criteria, not
to paste a generic request unchanged.

Sources:

- https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/get-started/best-practices
- https://docs.github.com/copilot/using-github-copilot/asking-github-copilot-questions-in-your-ide
- https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/responsible-use/chat

### Observation

After iteration 129, the learning path no longer framed the prompt pack as a
generic copy/paste artifact. Two daily-summary surfaces still used weaker
language: one rebuild completion criterion said the learner had written a
prompt that could be pasted directly to AI, and one next-step item told the
learner to copy the first prompt and ask only for a plan.

### Candidate Adopted

`daily summary prompt reuse source grounded rewrite`

The rebuild completion criterion now requires a source-evidence-backed AI
implementation handoff adapted to the learner's own goal. The daily-summary
next step now asks the learner to revise the first prompt for their goal and
request both a plan and verification criteria.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. This slice only updates
generated learner guidance and its compliance gate.

## Iteration 152 - Desktop Implementation Result Accept Guard

### Observation

The desktop implementation-result review already required evidence before
ACCEPT / REVISE / BLOCK, but the successful state still displayed `ACCEPT 후보`
and `근거 게이트 통과 · ACCEPT 판단 가능`. For a vibe-coding learner, that can
sound like AI output is accepted once boxes are checked. The product goal is
stricter: ACCEPT must remain a review candidate until changed files, command
results, failures/risks, and direct screen checks are recorded as learning
evidence.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result accept guard`

The desktop review flow now labels the successful state as `ACCEPT 검토 후보`.
It adds an explicit `ACCEPT 가드` summary to the decision panel and copied
prompts. The next action now says to leave evidence in the learning record
before moving to the next small improvement. Compliance coverage rejects the old
`ACCEPT 후보`, `ACCEPT 판단 가능`, and `학습 기록으로 남기고` shortcuts in the
desktop app.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. This slice only changes the
desktop review UI, generated prompts, styles, compliance coverage, and resume

## Iteration 153 - Source Cleanup Learner Confirmation Gate

### Observation

The source absorption ledger used `정리 검토 가능` after static-analysis gaps
were empty, and the study README said cleanup could be applied after a dry-run
reported READY and preserved artifacts existed. That was directionally correct,
but it could still be read as if READY itself granted deletion permission. The
user's goal is stricter: analyzed sources may be cleaned up for space only after
the app has documented what was absorbed, why no more investigation is needed,
and the learner confirms source links are no longer needed.

### Candidate Adopted

`source cleanup learner confirmation gate`

The source absorption ledger now says a source snapshot with no remaining static
research gaps is only a cleanup review candidate. It requires
source-absorption-ledger, session-verification, source-retention-guide, and
README.study.md to remain available, plus explicit learner confirmation that
source links no longer need to open. The study README now says cleanup deletes
only the generated session `source/` snapshot, not user-provided source.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. This slice only changes
generated cleanup guidance, tests, compliance coverage, and resume
documentation.

The AI prompt A/B lab correctly marks the vague prompt as BLOCK, but it called

source evidence, acceptance criteria, verification assertions, and learner
judgment.

assertions, and learner judgment checkpoints before sending the prompt to AI.

wording.
