## Completion Audit

| Requirement | Evidence | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Use AutoResearch without forgetting the product purpose | This note records the baseline/candidate/metric loop; `reference/source-retention-guide.html` says RepoTutor translates source into vibe-coding purpose, structure, terms, prompts, and verification boundaries. | PASS |
| Derive a needed improvement | Baseline section identifies the missing dedicated retention guide despite existing `dailySummaryReport.sourceHandling` and `source-snapshot-report.json`. | PASS |
| Improve the app, not just write a plan | `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts` now writes `reference/source-retention-guide.html` and marks it as a required teaching workspace artifact. | PASS |
| Keep raw source out of permanent product knowledge | Guide text says not to embed raw source as app knowledge; no automatic deletion or source cache was added. | PASS |
| Help a vibe-coding learner | The guide includes a copyable Korean prompt beginning with "나는 전문 개발자가 아니라 바이브코딩 개발자야..." and frames deletion as an evidence/verification decision. | PASS |
| Verify the candidate | Typecheck, targeted Vitest tests, compliance audit, build, and CLI smoke all passed; CLI smoke reported `verificationOk: true` and `verificationCheckedRequiredArtifacts: 728`. | PASS |
| Preserve cleanup state | No external source cache was created; there is nothing under `research/external-src/` to delete for this slice. | PASS |

## Iteration 2: Target Exposure

### Baseline

The first iteration made `reference/source-retention-guide.html` a required
teaching workspace artifact, but the guide was still easy to miss because it was
not listed as a first-class learning report target.

For the product goal, this matters because a vibe-coding learner should not need
to browse folder internals to find the source cleanup decision. The app should
surface the decision point as an explicit learning artifact: what was learned
from the source, what was not embedded, and what can be deleted after evidence is
retained.

### Candidate

Expose `source-retention-guide` as a named report target across the shared target
registry, desktop tabs, CLI open target list, and compliance audit.

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `source-retention-guide` with file path
    `../reference/source-retention-guide.html`
  - added the learner-facing title `소스 보존 판단`
  - added the direct command
    `repo-tutor open <session> --target source-retention-guide`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added a `소스 보존` report tab label so the desktop app can expose the
    guide from the report tab model
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `source-retention-guide` to `openTargetEntries`
  - updated CLI help text so learners can discover the target
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - checks the shared target entry title, file path, and terminal command
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - requires the target name, title, and direct command in the stateful teaching
    workspace audit
  - updates the headless CLI command audit to expect the new open target

### Same-Sample Verification

- `pnpm typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm build`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "creates teaching workspace state"`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "generates a complete study session"`: PASS
- CLI smoke session:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- study packages/core/tests/fixtures/simple-ts-app --mode quick --level beginner --format json --studies-root /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-retention-target-smoke --no-codex`
  - PASS
  - `verificationOk: true`
  - `verificationCheckedRequiredArtifacts: 728`
- CLI target list:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- open --list-targets --format json`
  - PASS
  - includes `source-retention-guide`
  - maps to `../reference/source-retention-guide.html`
- CLI direct target:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- open /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-retention-target-smoke/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc --target source-retention-guide --format json`
  - PASS
  - resolves to
    `/tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-retention-target-smoke/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc/reference/source-retention-guide.html`
- `git diff --check`: PASS
- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `allPassed: true`

### Not Adopted

- No new external source clone was added.
- No raw educational source was embedded into the product.
- No automatic deletion policy was added. The app now teaches and exposes the
  retention decision, but actual deletion remains an explicit user/operator
  action.

### Cleanup Status

No external source cache was created for this iteration. The retained artifact is
this analysis note plus the implemented source-retention target, tests, and audit
rules.

## Iteration 3: Codex Prompt Contract

### Baseline

Most generated lessons and product docs already state that RepoTutor is not a
traditional syntax tutor. The remaining weak point was the Codex SDK enrichment
prompt in `packages/core/src/pipeline.ts`, which still framed the task as
`beginner learning`.

That wording was too broad for the product mission. It could let AI-assisted
analysis drift into line-by-line code explanation instead of extracting the
architecture, role, term, prompt, and verification knowledge that a vibe-coding
learner needs.

### Candidate

Change the Codex prompt contract so AI assistance is explicitly aimed at a
vibe-coding learner who will brief, steer, and review AI implementation.

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/core/src/pipeline.ts`
  - replaced the generic `beginner learning` prompt with a contract focused on:
    purpose, architecture roles, essential terms, AI prompt strategy, and
    verification boundaries
  - states that the learner does not need language syntax memorization
- `packages/codex/src/index.ts`
  - updated the reusable `PromptTemplateRegistry.purposeTask` wording to the
    same vibe-coding learning contract
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies generated `codex/prompts.jsonl` contains the vibe-coding learner
    contract and no longer contains `beginner learning`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the Codex prompt contract strings to the shared core engine audit

### Same-Sample Verification

- `pnpm typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "generates a complete study session"`: PASS
- `pnpm build`: PASS
- `git diff --check`: PASS
- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `allPassed: true`

### Not Adopted

- No traditional coding curriculum or syntax drill was added.
- No external source was cloned or embedded.
- No automatic source deletion behavior was added.

### Cleanup Status

No external source cache was created for this iteration. The retained artifact is
this note plus the updated Codex prompt contract, tests, and audit rule.

## Iteration 4: Vibe-Coding Start Surface

### Baseline

RepoTutor already generated a broad `learning-path.html` and many readiness
reports. That is useful for a full review, but it is too much as the first screen
for a vibe-coding learner who is not trying to memorize programming syntax.

The product needs a narrower entry point: if the user gives a GitHub repository
or source folder, the first thing shown should be the purpose, architecture
rationale, AI-facing terms, first prompt, verification boundaries, and source
retention decision.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `vibe-coding-start` report that compresses the first 15
minutes of study into one HTML/Markdown surface, and expose it through the CLI,
desktop target grid, export manifest, required artifact verifier, tests, and
audit rules.

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - added `html/vibe-coding-start.html`
  - added the Start Here navigation card and export manifest entrypoint
  - linked to `overview`, `architecture`, `glossary`,
    `vibe-coding-prompt-pack`, `session-verification`, and
    `reference/source-retention-guide.html`
  - updated Teaching Workspace reference count from 2 to 3 and added a source
    retention reference card
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - added `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/session-verifier.ts`
  - made both HTML and Markdown start files required session artifacts
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `vibe-coding-start` as a shared learning report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target vibe-coding-start`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added a `시작` tab/target for the desktop app
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file generation, manifest entrypoint, start-page content, and
    shared target metadata
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - added the new target and start-page strings to the CLI, Tauri, lesson, HTML
    export, and teaching workspace audits

## Iteration 5: Source Absorption Ledger

### Baseline

The source retention guide explained why RepoTutor should not embed external
repositories as permanent app knowledge and how a learner can decide whether a
source snapshot can be cleaned up. It did not yet leave a per-session ledger
that answers the user's operational question: what did this app absorb from the
source, what no longer needs repeated investigation, and what still requires
additional evidence.

That gap matters because RepoTutor is for a vibe-coding learner, not a
traditional programming learner. The durable artifact should not be raw source
knowledge. It should be the source-derived purpose, architecture roles, AI
instruction terms, prompt sequence, verification boundaries, and cleanup
judgement.

### Candidate

Add `reference/source-absorption-ledger.html` and
`reference/source-absorption-ledger.md` to every teaching workspace. Expose the
HTML ledger through shared report targets, CLI `open`, the desktop target list,
the Teaching Workspace page, tests, and compliance audit.

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/source-absorption-ledger.html` and
    `reference/source-absorption-ledger.md` as required teaching artifacts
  - records what was absorbed into RepoTutor: purpose, architecture roles,
    AI-facing terms, implementation prompts, verification boundaries, and
    source retention judgement
  - separates `추가 조사 필요 여부` from already absorbed material by using
    runtime missing signals and uncovered important files
  - states that the original source is not embedded as app knowledge
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `source-absorption-ledger` as a shared learning report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target source-absorption-ledger`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the `흡수 Ledger` desktop tab label
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - linked the ledger from `teaching-workspace.html`
  - updated the Teaching Workspace reference count to 4
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies ledger file generation, shared target metadata, HTML/Markdown
    content, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - added the ledger target and required product-contract strings to the
    stateful teaching workspace audit

## Iteration 7: Dry-Run Source Prune Plan

### Baseline

RepoTutor now documents source retention and source absorption, but there was no
machine-readable cleanup checkpoint that could tell an operator whether a copied
session source snapshot is ready for manual pruning.

That gap matters for the product purpose. A vibe-coding learner should not keep
large source copies forever just because the app used them once. At the same
time, RepoTutor must not delete user-provided source automatically before the
learner has preserved the architectural briefing, prompt pack, glossary,
verification report, source absorption ledger, and source retention guide.

### Candidate

Add a dry-run `prune-source` CLI command that writes a cleanup plan without
deleting source files.

The command should answer:

- whether the source snapshot is still present
- how many source files and bytes would be eligible for manual cleanup
- whether session verification still passes
- whether the source absorption ledger, source retention guide, daily summary,
  prompt pack, verification reports, and source snapshot report are preserved
- whether cleanup is ready or blocked
- which report files were written

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - added source snapshot size/file counting
  - added preserved-artifact checks
  - calls `verifyStudySessionArtifacts` before declaring the plan ready
  - writes `analysis/source-prune-plan.json`
  - writes `markdown/source-prune-plan.md`
  - returns `applied: false` semantics through the CLI output
- `packages/core/src/index.ts`
  - exports the source prune planning API
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `prune-source <session-id-or-path> --format json|markdown`
  - added dry-run-only output
  - added doctor metadata for `pruneSource` and `sourcePruneDryRun`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies the plan is ready for a complete sample session
  - verifies the source directory still exists after planning
  - verifies both JSON and Markdown plan files are written
- `README.md`
  - documents the dry-run prune command in Quick Start and current scope
- `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - documents the cleanup policy and explicit non-deletion boundary
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds `prune-source` and source-prune report strings to the headless CLI
    compliance audit

## Iteration 8: Learner Role Contract

### Baseline

The previous iterations made source retention and source absorption explicit,
but a user-facing gap remained: the generated session did not have one durable
contract that answers the learner's core question, "If AI already knows how to
code, what do I actually need to learn?"

This matters because RepoTutor is not a traditional programming course. The
learner is a vibe-coding developer. The app should teach what the learner must
own: purpose, architecture rationale, responsibility boundaries, AI-facing
terms, prompt framing, and verification judgment. Concrete syntax details,
repetitive implementation, boilerplate, and many code edits can be delegated to
AI.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `learner-role-contract` reference artifact to every teaching
workspace.

The contract should separate:

- what the learner must understand
- what AI can implement
- what is not required in this session
- questions the learner must ask before AI writes code
- a copyable Korean role prompt

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/learner-role-contract.html`
  - added `reference/learner-role-contract.md`
  - added the contract to `TEACHING_WORKSPACE_REQUIRED_ARTIFACTS`
  - links the contract from `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md`
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `learner-role-contract` as a shared report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target learner-role-contract`
  - exposed the target in CLI help and doctor/open target metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the desktop report tab label `역할 계약`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the role contract from the main report, navigation, start page,
    teaching workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 4 to 5
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the role contract from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - treats the role contract HTML and Markdown as preserved artifacts before a
    source snapshot can be considered cleanup-ready
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file creation, content strings, target metadata, start-page links,
    source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md` and `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - document the role contract as part of the storage/source-retention model
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the new target and learner-role strings to compliance coverage

## Iteration 9: AI Output Review Rubric

### Baseline

RepoTutor already generated review prompts inside the Learning Journal and
Vibe-Coding Prompt Pack, but the user-facing workflow still lacked a dedicated
reference artifact for judging AI-generated output.

That gap matters because the learner is not trying to become a traditional
syntax-first developer. The learner should become capable of professional
judgment: deciding whether AI output preserves the product purpose,
architecture responsibility, source evidence, acceptance criteria, verification
boundary, and human decision points.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `ai-output-review-rubric` reference artifact to every teaching
workspace.

The rubric should teach the learner to classify AI output as:

- `PASS`: purpose and architecture fit, evidence and verification are clear
- `REVISE`: direction is usable but context, responsibility, evidence, or tests
  need tightening
- `BLOCK`: AI changed the product purpose, invented facts, skipped verification,
  or made human decisions without approval

### Adopted Into RepoTutor

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/ai-output-review-rubric.html`

  - links it from `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md`

  - added `ai-output-review-rubric` as a shared report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target ai-output-review-rubric`
  - exposed the target in CLI help and doctor/open target metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the desktop report tab label `AI 검토`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the rubric from the main report, global navigation, start page,
    teaching workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 5 to 6
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the rubric from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - treats the rubric HTML and Markdown as preserved artifacts before a source
    snapshot can be considered cleanup-ready
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file creation, content strings, target metadata, start-page links,

## Iteration 10 - Vibe-Coding Mastery Checklist

### Baseline

The app now explains the learner role and AI output review rubric, but the next
gap was readiness judgment. The user clarified that AI already has enough
general development knowledge, so source analysis should not look like source
embedding. The learner is a vibe-coding developer, not a traditional syntax
student. RepoTutor therefore needs a session artifact that asks whether the
learner can direct AI to build a similar app: purpose, architecture roles, terms,
prompt strategy, verification/review, and source cleanup judgment.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `vibe-coding-mastery-checklist` reference artifact to every
teaching workspace.

### Adopted Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/vibe-coding-mastery-checklist.html`
  - added `reference/vibe-coding-mastery-checklist.md`
  - marks both as required teaching workspace artifacts
  - links the checklist from `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md`
  - checklist uses READY / REVIEW / BLOCKED across product purpose,
    architecture, AI-facing terms, first implementation prompt, verification
    review, and source cleanup judgment
  - states explicitly that AI already has general development knowledge and
    source is temporary project-specific evidence
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `vibe-coding-mastery-checklist` as a shared report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target vibe-coding-mastery-checklist`
  - exposed the target in CLI help, `open --list-targets`, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the desktop report tab label `숙련도 체크`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the checklist from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 6 to 7
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the checklist from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - treats the checklist HTML and Markdown as preserved artifacts before a
    source snapshot can be considered cleanup-ready
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`

## Iteration 11 - Vibe-Coding Implementation Brief

### Baseline

The app had a prompt pack, learner role contract, AI output review rubric, and
mastery checklist. The remaining handoff gap was that the actual AI
implementation request was still spread across multiple reports. A vibe-coding
learner needs one compact artifact that says what to build first, why the
architecture matters, which source evidence to focus on, what counts as done,
and how to verify the AI-built slice.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `vibe-coding-implementation-brief` reference artifact to every
teaching workspace.

### Adopted Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/vibe-coding-implementation-brief.html`
  - added `reference/vibe-coding-implementation-brief.md`
  - marks both as required teaching workspace artifacts
  - builds the brief from product purpose, architecture rationale, first rebuild
    roadmap step, source focus, terms, acceptance criteria, verification plan,
    non-goals, and a copy/paste AI implementation prompt
  - links the brief from `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md`
  - records the brief in the source absorption ledger
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the implementation brief to source handling retained artifacts so the
    source-retention guide explains that this handoff has been absorbed
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves the implementation brief HTML and Markdown before source cleanup
    can be considered ready
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `vibe-coding-implementation-brief` as a shared report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target vibe-coding-implementation-brief`
  - exposed the target in CLI help, `open --list-targets`, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the desktop report tab label `구현 브리프`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the brief from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 7 to 8
  - adds the phrase `복사용 구현 프롬프트` to the Start Here implementation
    brief card so the offline HTML audit can prove the entry point exists
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the brief from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file creation, content strings, target metadata, start-page links,
    source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md` and `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - document that the implementation brief converts temporary source evidence
    into the first AI-built vertical slice rather than embedding source as
    permanent knowledge
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the new target and implementation-brief strings to compliance coverage

## Iteration 12 - AI Prompt Readiness Checklist

### Baseline

RepoTutor now creates a one-page implementation brief and an AI output review
rubric. The remaining gap was pre-flight prompt quality. A vibe-coding learner
can copy an implementation prompt, but still needs a way to judge whether the
prompt is scoped enough before sending it to AI. External references reinforced
this need: GitHub Copilot task guidance emphasizes clear problem statements,
acceptance criteria, and relevant files; Spec Kit-style workflows clarify
requirements before planning; promptfoo-style evaluation turns expected behavior
into assertions.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `ai-prompt-readiness-checklist` reference artifact to every
teaching workspace.

### Adopted Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/ai-prompt-readiness-checklist.html`
  - added `reference/ai-prompt-readiness-checklist.md`
  - marks both as required teaching workspace artifacts
  - checks problem/goal, related source evidence, small vertical slice,
    acceptance criteria, verification assertions, and failure reporting
  - uses READY / REVISE / BLOCK so the learner can improve the prompt before
    AI starts coding
  - links the checklist from `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md`
  - records the prompt readiness artifact in the source absorption ledger
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds prompt readiness to source handling retained artifacts so
    `source-retention-guide.html` shows this guidance was preserved
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves the prompt readiness HTML and Markdown before source cleanup can
    be considered ready
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `ai-prompt-readiness-checklist` as a shared report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target ai-prompt-readiness-checklist`
  - exposed the target in CLI help, `open --list-targets`, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the desktop report tab label `프롬프트 준비도`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the checklist from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 8 to 9
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the checklist from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file creation, content strings, target metadata, start-page links,
    source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document that prompt-readiness checking is an absorbed external best
    practice, not a reason to keep source repositories embedded
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the new target and prompt readiness strings to compliance coverage

## Iteration 13 - AI Prompt A/B Lab

### Baseline

RepoTutor already tells a vibe-coding learner whether a prompt is ready before
AI starts implementation. The remaining gap was contrast. A non-professional
developer can still ask "make something like this app" and miss why source
evidence, scope, acceptance criteria, and verification assertions matter. The
app needed a learner-facing A/B comparison that shows the difference between a
vague one-line request and a source-grounded implementation prompt.

### Candidate

Add a first-class `ai-prompt-ab-lab` reference artifact to every teaching
workspace. The artifact should not run live prompt experiments or embed source
code as permanent knowledge. It should teach the learner how to compare prompt
quality before handing work to AI.

### Adopted Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - added `reference/ai-prompt-ab-lab.html`
  - added `reference/ai-prompt-ab-lab.md`
  - marks both as required teaching workspace artifacts
  - compares `프롬프트 A` with `프롬프트 B`
  - explains that AI already has general development knowledge, and source is
    useful as evidence for purpose, responsibility boundaries, scope, and
    verification
  - includes an `A/B 판정표` covering purpose clarity, source evidence, task
    size, verification, and failure reporting
  - includes a `복사용 A/B 평가 프롬프트`
  - links the lab from `RESOURCES.md` and `NOTES.md`
  - records the lab in the source absorption ledger
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves the A/B lab HTML and Markdown before source cleanup can be
    considered ready
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - added `ai-prompt-ab-lab` as a shared report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - added `open --target ai-prompt-ab-lab`
  - exposed the target in CLI help, `open --list-targets`, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - added the desktop report tab label `프롬프트 A/B 랩`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the lab from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 9 to 10
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the lab from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the lab to daily-summary source handling retained artifacts
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file creation, content strings, target metadata, start-page links,
    source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document the A/B lab as a source-grounded prompt training feature
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds the new target and stateful teaching strings to compliance coverage

## Iteration 14 - Architecture Principle Playbook

### Baseline

The app already emphasized that learners do not need to memorize language
syntax and that source snapshots are temporary evidence. However, the Start
Here flow still jumped from architecture rationale into terms and prompts
without a dedicated learner artifact for architecture principles: why the
structure exists, how to ask AI to preserve responsibility boundaries, what
goes wrong, and how to verify the result.

### Candidate

Add a source-backed `architecture-principle-playbook` reference artifact that
teaches architecture judgment for vibe-coding learners. The artifact must not
embed source code as permanent app knowledge. It should convert the analyzed
project's purpose, responsibility boundaries, core file roles, terms, and
verification questions into reusable AI-direction principles.

### Adopted

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - writes `reference/architecture-principle-playbook.html`
  - writes `reference/architecture-principle-playbook.md`
  - adds the playbook to required artifacts, RESOURCES, NOTES, and source
    absorption ledger output
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves both architecture principle artifacts before source cleanup can be
    considered ready
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - adds the `architecture-principle-playbook` learning target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - adds `open --target architecture-principle-playbook`
  - exposes the target in CLI help, `open --list-targets`, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds the desktop report tab label `아키텍처 원리`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the playbook from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 10 to 11
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the playbook from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the playbook to daily-summary source handling retained artifacts
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies file creation, content strings, target metadata, Start Here links,
    source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document the playbook as architecture judgment training rather than syntax
    training
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the new target and raise `audit:brief` to 14
    iterations

## Iteration 15 - Source-to-Build Interview

### Baseline

The app already made clear that RepoTutor is for vibe-coding learners, not a
traditional syntax-first developer curriculum. However, the learner still needed
a dedicated self-explanation step before handing work to AI: purpose, structure,
responsibility boundaries, terms, first vertical slice, and verification
questions had to be answered in the learner's own words.

External learning-science intake reinforced this gap:

- Prompted free self-explanations can improve source-code comprehension:
  https://ceur-ws.org/Vol-3051/CSEDM_13.pdf
- Socratic tutoring for source-code comprehension uses guiding questions about
  concepts and major code segments rather than direct answer delivery:
  https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7334736/
- Retrieval practice supports learning by asking learners to recall and explain
  knowledge instead of only rereading it:
  https://psychology.ucsd.edu/undergraduate-program/undergraduate-resources/academic-writing-resources/effective-studying/retrieval-practice.html

### Candidate

Add a source-backed `source-to-build-interview` artifact. It should not teach
line-by-line coding or permanently embed the source. It should turn analyzed
source evidence into interview cards that ask the learner to explain what they
want to build and then ask AI to check the answer against source evidence.

### Adopted

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - writes `reference/source-to-build-interview.html`
  - writes `reference/source-to-build-interview.md`
  - adds required artifacts, RESOURCES, NOTES, and source absorption ledger
    coverage
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves both interview artifacts before source cleanup can be considered
    ready
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - adds the `source-to-build-interview` learning target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - adds `open --target source-to-build-interview`
  - exposes the target in CLI help, target listing, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds the desktop report tab label `소스-빌드 인터뷰`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the interview from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 11 to 12
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - links the interview from `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the interview to daily-summary retained artifacts
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`

## Iteration 16 - Similar-App Transfer Map

### Baseline

The app now had a source-to-build interview, but the learner still needed a
clear transfer step: which parts of the reference source should remain as
principles, and which parts should change for a new product. Without that step,
a vibe-coding learner could still treat source analysis as copying or source
embedding instead of guided adaptation.

External intake reinforced this gap:

- Applications of analogical transfer for programming education:
  https://terpconnect.umd.edu/~weintrop/papers/KaoEtAl_TOCE_2022.pdf
- Prompt engineering as a 21st century skill:
  https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/education/articles/10.3389/feduc.2024.1366434/full
- Epistemic debt and metacognitive scripts for vibe coding:
  https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20206

### Candidate

Add a `similar-app-transfer-map` artifact that explicitly separates source
principles from new-app decisions. The learner should be able to ask AI for
`KEEP / ADAPT / ASK AI / VERIFY` rather than asking AI to copy the original
repository or study syntax line by line.

### Adopted

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - writes `reference/similar-app-transfer-map.html`
  - writes `reference/similar-app-transfer-map.md`
  - adds required artifacts, RESOURCES, NOTES, and source absorption ledger
    coverage
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves both transfer-map artifacts before source cleanup can be
    considered ready
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - adds the `similar-app-transfer-map` learning target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - adds `open --target similar-app-transfer-map`
  - exposes the target in CLI help, target listing, and doctor metadata
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds the desktop report tab label `전이 지도`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the transfer map from global navigation, index, Start Here, Teaching
    Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 12 to 13
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds `비슷한 앱 전이 지도` to `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the transfer map to daily-summary retained artifacts
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies artifact creation, content strings, target metadata, Start Here
    links, source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document that source is temporary evidence for transfer learning, not a

## Iteration 17 - AI Implementation Conversation Loop

### Baseline Gap

RepoTutor had already added source retention guidance, an implementation brief,
AI prompt readiness checks, a prompt A/B lab, and a similar-app transfer map.
That answered what to learn from a source project before asking AI to build.
The remaining gap was after AI returns code: a vibe-coding learner still needs a
repeatable loop for reviewing the AI output, deciding the next question, asking
for minimal changes, and separating verified results from unverified claims.

### External Research Inputs

- Scaffolding Metacognition in Programming Education through Student-AI
  Interaction Analysis:
  `https://arxiv.org/html/2511.04144v1`
- Plan More, Debug Less: Applying Metacognitive Theory to AI-Assisted
  Programming Education:
  `https://arxiv.org/html/2509.03171v1`
- Reflective AI Programming Lab: Towards a Pedagogical Framework for
  AI-Generated-Code Collaboration:
  `https://ceur-ws.org/Vol-3879/AIxEDU2024_paper_13.pdf`

### Absorbed Principle

For RepoTutor's product goal, the useful principle is not syntax memorization.
The learner should practice planning, monitoring, evaluating, revising, and
verifying AI-generated code. The app should therefore teach the user to operate
as a product/architecture/review owner: define intent, inspect what AI changed,
check source evidence, request small corrections, run or demand verification,
and only then move to the next prompt.

### Candidate Adopted

`ai-implementation-loop`

The new artifact turns AI-assisted implementation into a six-step conversation
loop:

- `PLAN`: define the smallest useful implementation request.
- `OBSERVE`: inspect what AI says it changed.
- `CHECK`: compare the result against source evidence and the transfer map.
- `REVISE`: ask for the smallest correction when assumptions or scope drift.
- `VERIFY`: separate executed checks from unrun claims.
- `NEXT`: convert the current result into the next focused question.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - writes `reference/ai-implementation-loop.html`
  - writes `reference/ai-implementation-loop.md`
  - adds the artifact to resources and learner notes
  - updates source absorption status so the app records that this principle has
    been absorbed and does not need repeated research unless new dialogue-log
    evaluation experiments are being considered
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves the AI implementation loop during source cleanup checks
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - exposes `ai-implementation-loop` as a learning report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - adds `repo-tutor open <session> --target ai-implementation-loop`
  - adds the target to CLI help/listing
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds the desktop report tab label `구현 루프`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the implementation loop from global navigation, index, Start Here,
    Teaching Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 13 to 14
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds `AI 구현 대화 루프` to `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the loop to daily-summary retained artifacts
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies artifact creation, content strings, target metadata, Start Here
    links, source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document that RepoTutor uses source as temporary evidence and teaches the
    learner how to direct and verify AI implementation rather than memorizing
    language-level syntax
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the new target and raise `audit:brief` to 17
    iterations

## Iteration 18 - Learner Goal Alignment Import

### Baseline Gap

RepoTutor could already derive source-backed purpose, architecture, transfer
maps, implementation briefs, prompt readiness checks, A/B prompt comparisons,
and AI implementation loops. The missing product step was the learner's own
current intent. A vibe-coding learner often has an issue, PRD, or rough AI
prompt before implementation. Without importing that artifact, RepoTutor could
teach the source but could not compare "what I want to build now" against the
source-backed guide.

### External Research Inputs

- GitHub Copilot task best practices:
  `https://docs.github.com/copilot/how-tos/agents/copilot-coding-agent/best-practices-for-using-copilot-to-work-on-tasks`
- GitHub Spec Kit documentation:
  `https://github.github.com/spec-kit/`
- Prompts Blend Requirements and Solutions: From Intent to Implementation in
  AI-Assisted Software Development:
  `https://arxiv.org/html/2603.16348v1`

### Absorbed Principle

The learner should not learn traditional coding syntax just to use a source
repo. The learner should keep intent, constraints, acceptance criteria, source
evidence, and verification boundaries explicit before asking AI to implement.
RepoTutor therefore needs an optional learner-brief input that stays separate
from the source snapshot and is used only to compare the learner's current
PRD/issue/prompt against source-grounded guidance.

### Candidate Adopted

`learner-goal-alignment`

The new artifact compares an optional learner PRD, issue, or AI prompt against
source-backed criteria:

- problem and user
- architecture responsibilities
- first vertical slice
- relevant source evidence
- acceptance criteria
- verification and stop conditions
- human/AI role boundary

Rows are marked `ACCEPT`, `CLARIFY`, `REWRITE`, or `BLOCK` so a broad "make
this app" prompt does not proceed to AI implementation until missing intent or
verification criteria are repaired.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/pipeline.ts`
  - adds `learnerBriefPath` to `StudyOptions`
  - reads an optional learner brief up to 200 KB
  - copies it into `inputs/learner-brief.md`
  - passes it to the teaching workspace renderer without merging it into the
    source snapshot
- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - writes `reference/learner-goal-alignment.html`
  - writes `reference/learner-goal-alignment.md`
  - adds imported-brief metadata, source-grounded gap checks, and a copyable
    goal-alignment prompt
  - adds the feature to RESOURCES, NOTES, required artifacts, and source
    absorption ledger
- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - preserves the learner goal alignment reports during cleanup checks
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - exposes `learner-goal-alignment` as a learning report target
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - adds `--learner-brief <file>`
  - adds `learnerGoalAlignmentHtml` to study output
  - exposes `repo-tutor open <session> --target learner-goal-alignment`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds the desktop report tab label `목표 정렬`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - links the goal alignment report from navigation, index, Start Here,
    Teaching Workspace, and export manifest entrypoints
  - updates the teaching workspace reference count from 14 to 15
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds `학습자 목표 정렬` to `markdown/vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/core/src/scanner.ts`
  - adds the report to daily-summary retained artifacts
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies default artifact creation
  - verifies learner brief import and `inputs/learner-brief.md` copy
  - verifies content strings, target metadata, Start Here links,
    source-prune preservation, and RESOURCES/NOTES links
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document the optional brief flow and clarify that the brief is not merged
    into the source snapshot
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the new target and raise `audit:brief` to 18
    iterations

## Iteration 19 - Explicit Source Prune Apply

### Baseline

RepoTutor could prove that a generated study session had enough retained
learning artifacts to make the copied `source/` snapshot disposable, but the
CLI stopped at a dry-run plan. That left the learner with a correct retention
decision but no safe one-command way to reclaim disk space.

### Candidate Adopted

`prune-source --apply --confirm DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT`

The CLI now supports an explicit destructive mode that:

- re-runs the prune plan and requires `applyReady: true`
- refuses to remove any path other than the generated session `source/`
  directory
- requires the exact confirmation token `DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT`
- records `analysis/source-prune-applied.json`
- writes `SOURCE-PRUNED.md` so future readers know why source links may no
  longer open

This matches the product direction: source is temporary evidence, not permanent
app knowledge. The preserved artifacts are the learner-facing value: purpose,
architecture roles, terminology, prompt guidance, verification boundaries,
transfer maps, learner goal alignment, and source absorption decisions.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - adds `SourcePruneApplyResult`
  - adds `applySourcePrunePlan`
  - adds `sourcePruneApplyMarkdown`
  - writes apply evidence and a tombstone after deleting only the generated
    session source snapshot
- `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
  - adds `prune-source --apply --confirm DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT`
  - returns `applied: true` only after the delete and evidence write succeed
  - keeps the default command dry-run only
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies that apply succeeds only after the preserved artifact gate passes
  - verifies source removal, apply report, tombstone, and removed file/byte
    counters
- `README.md` and `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - document the explicit cleanup command and its generated evidence files
- `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - moves source retention cleanup from an unimplemented candidate to current
    CLI coverage, leaving UI controls as future work
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the apply gate and raise `audit:brief` to 19
    iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core build`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "applies source prune only after the preserved artifact gate passes"`:
  PASS
- CLI study smoke:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- study packages/core/tests/fixtures/simple-ts-app --mode quick --level beginner --format json --studies-root /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke/studies --no-codex`
  - PASS
  - session root:
    `/tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke/studies/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc`
  - `verificationOk: true`
  - `verificationCheckedRequiredArtifacts: 754`
- CLI apply without confirmation:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- prune-source /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke/studies/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc --apply --format json`
  - PASS fail-closed
  - exit status: 1
  - message: `prune-source --apply requires --confirm DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT.`
- CLI apply with confirmation:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- prune-source /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke/studies/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc --apply --confirm DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT --format json`
  - PASS

  - `tombstone_present`

### Not Adopted

- No deletion of the user's original GitHub checkout or local source folder was
  added.
- No broad cleanup outside a generated study session was added.
- No deletion is allowed when the preserved artifact or verification gate fails.
- No source snapshot is deleted without the exact confirmation token.

study session's generated `source/` snapshot under
`/tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke`. No project source and
no original user source folder was removed.

## Iteration 20 - Tombstone-Aware Pruned Session Verification

### Baseline

After Iteration 19, explicit cleanup worked, but a cleaned session became
confusing on the next verification pass. Running:

`pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- verify-session /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke/studies/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc --format json`

failed with `ok: false` because `verifyEvidenceIndexReport` treated every
missing `source/...` evidence link as an error even when
`analysis/source-prune-applied.json` and `SOURCE-PRUNED.md` proved the source
snapshot had been intentionally pruned.

### Candidate Adopted

`tombstone-aware evidence verification`

The evidence verifier now detects a pruned session only when both files exist:

- `analysis/source-prune-applied.json`
- `SOURCE-PRUNED.md`

In that state, source evidence paths under `source/` are counted as skipped
pruned source links instead of failures. Lesson links, lesson anchors, HTML
export integrity, required artifacts, and unsafe paths still use the normal
fail-closed checks.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/evidence.ts`
  - adds `sourcePruned` to `EvidenceVerificationResult`
  - adds `skippedPrunedSourceLinks`
  - adds `isSourcePruned`
  - skips only safe `source/` evidence links when the tombstone and apply
    report are present
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - extends the source-prune apply test to verify that pruned evidence and
    pruned session checks pass
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document that pruned sessions stay verifiable after explicit cleanup
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for tombstone-aware verification and raise
    `audit:brief` to 20 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- Baseline CLI verification after cleanup:
  - before fix: FAIL
  - `checks.evidenceIndex: false`
  - failures included `missing-source-path` and `missing-source-href`
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "applies source prune only after the preserved artifact gate passes"`:
  PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core build`: PASS
- CLI verification after fix:
  `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev -- verify-session /tmp/repotutor-autoresearch-source-prune-apply-smoke/studies/2026-06-10/local__simple-ts-app__main__33f74ebc --format json`
  - PASS
  - `ok: true`
  - `checks.evidenceIndex: true`
  - `evidenceIndex.sourcePruned: true`
  - `evidenceIndex.skippedPrunedSourceLinks: 18`
  - `failures: []`

### Not Adopted

- No missing source links are ignored unless both tombstone files exist.
- No lesson link, lesson anchor, HTML export, or required artifact failure is
  downgraded.
- No broad missing-file suppression was added.

### Cleanup Status

No additional source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. The same `/tmp`
smoke session from Iteration 19 was reused to prove the verification regression
and fix.

## Iteration 21 - Desktop Source Retention Controls

### Baseline

Source cleanup was available through the CLI, but the desktop app only exposed
study, session list, quiz, and report preview flows. A vibe-coding learner using
the app UI could read the source retention guide but still had to switch to a
terminal to check whether cleanup was ready or to apply the confirmed cleanup.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop retention controls`

The desktop app now exposes the same retention contract as the CLI:

- check the source prune plan for the current session
- show whether cleanup is ready, blocked, or already pruned
- show source snapshot size and blockers
- require the exact `DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT` token before apply
- delete only the generated session `source/` snapshot through the same core
  `applySourcePrunePlan` path

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar/bridge.ts`
  - adds `sourcePrunePlan`
  - adds `applySourcePrune`
  - delegates to `writeSourcePrunePlan` and `applySourcePrunePlan`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - adds Tauri commands `source_prune_plan` and `apply_source_prune`
  - uses sidecar first, with CLI fallback to `repo-tutor prune-source`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `SourcePruneResponse`
  - adds `refreshSourceRetention`
  - adds `applySourceRetentionCleanup`
  - shows a source retention panel for the current session
  - prompts for `DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT` before destructive cleanup
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `retention-panel` and `retention-actions`
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document Desktop UI retention controls
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the desktop retention surface and raise
    `audit:brief` to 21 iterations

- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS
- `cargo check` in `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS
- Playwright desktop-width smoke against Vite `http://localhost:1420/`: PASS

## Iteration 22 - Desktop Learner Brief Goal Alignment

### Baseline

The CLI could import a learner PRD, issue, or current AI prompt through
`--learner-brief`, but the desktop app still only accepted the source target,
mode, and learner level. That left a mismatch with the product goal: RepoTutor
is for a vibe-coding learner who wants to make something similar with AI, not a
traditional developer memorizing syntax.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop learner brief`

The desktop app now accepts the learner's rough goal, PRD, issue, or AI prompt
and sends it into the same source-grounded goal alignment flow as the CLI.
Source remains temporary evidence; the learner brief is intent used to produce
architecture, responsibility, prompt, acceptance-criteria, and verification
guidance.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/pipeline.ts`
  - adds `learnerBriefText` to `StudyOptions`
  - accepts either `learnerBriefPath` or inline text, not both
  - caps inline text at 200 KB
  - stores inline input as `inputs/learner-brief.md` with
    `sourcePath: "inline:learner-brief"`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies inline learner brief text generates
    `reference/learner-goal-alignment.html`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/sidecar/bridge.ts`
  - forwards `learnerBriefText` to `runStudy`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
  - adds `learner_brief_text` to `study_source`
  - passes it through sidecar first
  - writes a temporary `repotutor-tauri-learner-brief-<pid>.md` file only for
    CLI fallback
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds the `내 목표 / PRD / 이슈 / AI 프롬프트` textarea
  - passes the value to `study_source`
- `README.md`, `docs/product/storage-model.md`, and
  `docs/research/vibe-coding-best-practices.md`
  - document Desktop learner brief input as source-grounded goal alignment for
    vibe-coding, not a traditional development lesson
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for desktop learner brief goal alignment and raise
    `audit:brief` to 22 iterations

## Iteration 23 - Desktop Command Input Accessible Labels

### Baseline

The desktop learner brief UI worked by CSS selectors, but the source target and
learner brief controls were rendered as a `<label>` followed by an input inside
a plain `<div>`. Playwright `getByLabel` could not find the source field. That
is a real defect for a Korean-first desktop workbench because the source target
and learner goal are the primary path for a vibe-coding learner.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop command input accessible labels`

The desktop source target input and learner brief textarea now have explicit
`htmlFor`/`id` connections:

- `htmlFor="source-target-input"` with `id="source-target-input"`
- `htmlFor="learner-brief-textarea"` with `id="learner-brief-textarea"`

This keeps the visible layout unchanged while allowing browser automation and
assistive technology to address the controls by their real labels.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - connects `GitHub URL / 로컬 폴더 / ZIP / SKILL.md` to the source input
  - connects `내 목표 / PRD / 이슈 / AI 프롬프트` to the learner brief textarea
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for accessible command input labels and raise
    `audit:brief` to 23 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS
- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- Playwright desktop UI smoke against Vite `http://localhost:1420/`: PASS
  - `getByLabel("GitHub URL / 로컬 폴더 / ZIP / SKILL.md")` filled the source
    input
  - `getByLabel("내 목표 / PRD / 이슈 / AI 프롬프트")` filled the learner brief
    textarea
  - `study_source` payload preserved both `source` and `learnerBriefText`
  - screenshot: `/tmp/repotutor-desktop-accessible-labels.png`

### Not Adopted

- No visual layout change was made.
- No new instructional copy was added to the first-screen command band.
- No source snapshot retention behavior changed.

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. The Playwright smoke used a
mocked Tauri bridge and did not create a real study session.

## Iteration 169 - Desktop Implementation Result Review Clipboard Judgment Candidate Wording

### Baseline

The desktop implementation-result review copy log said that the copied prompt
could judge the first vertical slice as ACCEPT / REVISE / BLOCK. That phrasing
was weaker than the product model because copying a prompt is not enough to
complete a learner judgment. The learner still needs evidence, verification
records, and explicit review before accepting or revising AI output.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result review clipboard judgment candidate wording`

The clipboard success log now says the learner checks evidence first and creates
an ACCEPT / REVISE / BLOCK judgment candidate. The audit gate also forbids the
older direct-judgment wording from returning.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - changes the implementation-result review clipboard success log to require
    evidence review before creating a judgment candidate
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the new wording and raise `audit:brief` to 169
    iterations

## Iteration 165 - Desktop Implementation Result Next Action Prompt

### Observation

The desktop AI implementation-result next-action prompt still used immediate
action language: `바로 실행할 다음 행동` and `바로 보낼 AI 프롬프트`. That wording
could make a vibe-coding learner treat the generated follow-up as a command to
send directly, even though RepoTutor's goal is to keep the learner responsible
for reviewing evidence, verification records, and the next small slice before
asking AI to continue.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result next action prompt`

The desktop next-action prompt now asks for a learner-reviewed action and a
learner-reviewed AI prompt:

- `학습자가 검토 후 다듬어 실행할 다음 행동`
- `학습자가 검토 후 다듬어 보낼 AI 프롬프트`

The clipboard success log also says the learner can review and refine the next
instruction before sending it.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - replaces immediate next-action wording with learner review wording
  - replaces immediate AI prompt wording with learner review wording
  - updates the next-action clipboard log to say the instruction should be
    reviewed and refined
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add regression coverage for the new desktop next-action wording and raise
    `audit:brief` to 165 iterations

## Iteration 166 - Teaching Workspace Mastery Review Progression Wording

### Observation

The generated `reference/vibe-coding-mastery-checklist.html` and Markdown used
REVIEW wording that still sounded like lightweight permission to proceed:
`한 번 더 요약하거나 질문하면 진행할 수 있습니다`. The self-evaluation prompt
also asked AI to turn gaps into `바로 다음 질문 3개`. That was weaker than the
current product goal: a vibe-coding learner should reinforce evidence and answer
review questions before treating a prompt or next slice as ready.

### Candidate Adopted

`teaching workspace mastery review progression wording`

The mastery checklist now says REVIEW means the learner must answer review
questions and reinforce evidence before the work becomes a progression
candidate. The self-evaluation prompt now asks for questions the learner should
review and answer:

- `검토 질문에 답하고 근거를 보강해야 진행 후보가 됩니다`
- `학습자가 검토 후 답해야 할 다음 질문 3개`

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - updates the REVIEW meaning in the generated mastery checklist
  - updates the HTML and Markdown self-evaluation prompt wording
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the new REVIEW and question wording
  - rejects the old progress-permission and immediate-question wording
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add regression coverage for the new mastery checklist wording and raise
    `audit:brief` to 166 iterations

## Iteration 167 - Source Absorption Prompt Pack Review Wording

### Observation

The generated source absorption ledger already records which learning artifacts
were preserved and when no further research is needed. Its implementation prompt
row still said `AI에게 바로 줄 orient/architect/plan/review 프롬프트는 prompt
pack에 남았습니다.` That wording was too close to a paste-ready handoff and did
not match the current learner model, where prompts are evidence-backed material
the learner reviews and adapts before sending to AI.

### Candidate Adopted

`source absorption prompt pack review wording`

The source absorption ledger now says the prompt pack preserves:

- `학습자가 검토 후 다듬어 보낼 orient/architect/plan/review 프롬프트`

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - updates the implementation-prompt row in the source absorption ledger data
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the new learner-review wording in HTML and Markdown ledgers
  - rejects the old immediate-handoff wording
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add regression coverage for the new source absorption wording and raise
    `audit:brief` to 167 iterations

## Iteration 119 - Main Glossary Prompt Review Cards

### Baseline

`reference/glossary.html` already explained that glossary terms are not
developer exam words, but the main `html/glossary.html` cards still mostly
showed definition, project meaning, example, and related terms. That left the
most visible glossary page weaker for a vibe-coding learner who needs terms as
AI prompt and review language rather than syntax memorization material.

`main glossary prompt review cards`

The main glossary now renders each term as an AI-facing prompt/review card:
`AI에게 지시할 문장`, `AI 출력 리뷰 질문`, and `외우지 말 것`. The Markdown
glossary keeps the same guidance so CLI and offline readers get the same
vibe-coding learning boundary.

## Iteration 118 - Source Prune Applied Learner Cleanup Decision

### Baseline

The dry-run prune plan already recorded `sourceKnowledgePolicy` and
`learnerCleanupDecision`, but the durable `analysis/source-prune-applied.json`
written after cleanup only retained the policy and learning artifact list. That
left the post-cleanup record weaker than the pre-cleanup plan for a vibe-coding
learner who needs to prove why the original source snapshot no longer has to be
kept.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune applied learner cleanup decision`

The apply result now keeps the same learner cleanup decision object as the plan,
and the `SOURCE-PRUNED.md` tombstone renders Korean apply/hold conditions. This
keeps the cleanup proof aligned with the product rule: `source/` is temporary
project evidence for purpose, architecture reasons, role terms, AI prompts,
acceptance criteria, and verification boundaries, not embedded AI development
knowledge.

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`

  - renders `정리 판단 조건`, `정리 가능 조건`, and `정리 보류 조건` in
    `SOURCE-PRUNED.md`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the applied JSON and tombstone keep the cleanup decision
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - accepts the applied cleanup decision in the desktop response type
- `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - documents that applied cleanup records preserve the same policy and
    decision context

## Iteration 115 - CLI/Sidecar Dev Runtime Deps Freshness Guard

### Baseline

During the iteration 114 CLI JSON smoke, Vitest saw the new TypeScript source
fields but `pnpm --filter @repotutor/cli dev` initially emitted stale JSON
because `@repotutor/core` is imported through the package `dist` export. That
can confuse the learning app workflow: a source-retention improvement may be
implemented, but the CLI or sidecar may still read the previous built package
until runtime dependencies are rebuilt.

### Candidate Adopted

`cli sidecar dev runtime deps freshness guard`

The root package now exposes `build:runtime-deps`, which builds
`@repotutor/shared`, `@repotutor/codex`, `@repotutor/html`, and
`@repotutor/core` in sorted workspace order. The CLI `dev` script and desktop
`sidecar` script run that guard before `tsx`, so local dev commands refresh the
runtime package exports before importing them.

### App Changes

- `package.json`
  - adds `build:runtime-deps`
  - raises `audit:brief` to 115 iterations
- `apps/cli/package.json`
  - changes `dev` to `pnpm -w build:runtime-deps && tsx src/index.ts`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/package.json`
  - changes `sidecar` to `pnpm -w build:runtime-deps && tsx sidecar/bridge.ts`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds `cli sidecar dev runtime deps freshness guard`

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this implementation step.

## Iteration 114 - Source Prune Plan Machine Readable Learner Cleanup Decision

### Baseline

The markdown prune plan already explained the learner cleanup decision, but
JSON consumers still had to infer the same policy from prose or
`recommendedAction`. That left desktop prompts, future automation, and any
external UI with weaker evidence for why source/ is temporary project evidence
and when cleanup should be held.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune plan machine readable learner cleanup decision`

`source-prune-plan.json` now includes `sourceKnowledgePolicy` and
`learnerCleanupDecision`. The structured decision records:

- `sourcePurpose`: source/ is project-specific evidence for purpose,
  architecture reasons, role terms, AI prompts, acceptance criteria, and
  verification boundaries
- `applyWhen`: conditions such as session verification and preserved evidence
  bundle passing
- `holdWhen`: conditions such as missing verification commands or human review
  criteria
- `retainedEvidence`: the preserved evidence bundle paths

Desktop retention prompts now include `정리 가능 조건` and `정리 보류 조건` from
that JSON.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - adds `SourcePruneLearnerCleanupDecision`
  - adds `sourceKnowledgePolicy` and `learnerCleanupDecision` to dry-run plans
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the new JSON fields in the plan report
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - reads the structured decision and includes it in source retention prompts
- `docs/product/storage-model.md`
  - documents the dry-run JSON fields
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 114 iterations

## Iteration 85 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Readiness

### Baseline

Iteration 84 let the learner write an implementation evidence note, but the app
did not say whether the note was strong enough to support a `BLOCK`, `REVISE`,
or `ACCEPT` judgment. A vibe-coding learner needs immediate feedback when the
note is missing changed files, commands, remaining failures/risks, or manually
observed screens.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result evidence readiness`

The desktop implementation result review panel now derives a `근거 준비도`
summary from the evidence note. It checks for changed files, executed commands,
failure/risk status, and direct screen/action confirmation. The readiness
summary is shown in the UI and included in review, revision, record, and
next-action prompts so verdicts are backed by visible evidence quality.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceChecks`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceReadyCount`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceSummary`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceMissingLabels`
  - renders `AI 구현 결과 근거 준비도`
  - resets result review state and evidence text when a new session is selected
    or a new study starts
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-result-evidence-readiness` status-chip styling
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 85 iterations

## Iteration 86 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Repair Prompt

### Baseline

Iteration 85 showed whether an implementation evidence note was complete, but a
vibe-coding learner still had to manually translate missing evidence into an AI
request. That leaves a gap between seeing `근거 보강 필요` and knowing how to
ask AI to collect changed files, command results, remaining risks, and direct
screen/action checks before `ACCEPT / REVISE / BLOCK`.

`desktop implementation result evidence repair prompt`

프롬프트` below the evidence readiness chips. It turns missing evidence
dimensions into a copyable AI request and explicitly tells AI to gather evidence
only, not start a new feature or broad refactor.

  missing gap, confirmed full evidence raises readiness to
  `근거 준비도 4/4 · 판정 근거 충분`, and confirmed `근거 보강 프롬프트 복사`
  writes the visible repair prompt to the clipboard. Screenshot:

## Iteration 87 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Acceptance Gate

### Baseline

Iteration 86 made missing evidence copyable as a repair prompt, but the result
decision still depended mainly on the review checklist. A learner could check
all review criteria and see an `ACCEPT 후보` summary even when the evidence note
was still missing changed files, command results, failure/risk status, or direct
screen/action confirmation.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result evidence acceptance gate`

The desktop result decision now treats evidence readiness as an acceptance gate.
If `근거 준비도` is incomplete, the visible decision stays at `BLOCK 검토`, the
next action becomes `근거 보강 프롬프트로 증거 먼저 수집`, and completed review
criteria show `근거 보강 후 ACCEPT 판단` instead of implying that the result is
ready to accept.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceReady`
  - gates `implementationResultReviewDecision` and
    `implementationResultReviewNextAction` on evidence readiness
  - adds `근거 게이트` and missing evidence details to record and next-action
    prompts
  - renders the decision panel's `근거` status as `통과` or `차단`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - changes the result decision grid to auto-fit the added evidence status
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 87 iterations

## Iteration 88 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Blocker Summary

### Baseline

Iteration 87 correctly blocked `ACCEPT 후보` when evidence was incomplete, but
the learner still had to read the readiness chips or long prompts to understand
which evidence dimension was blocking the decision. A vibe-coding learner needs
the blocked reason and next action immediately in the decision panel.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result evidence blocker summary`

The desktop result decision panel now shows a first-row evidence blocker
summary. When evidence is incomplete it says which dimensions are missing and
tells the learner to copy the evidence repair prompt before any acceptance or
next feature work. When evidence is complete it changes to a pass summary.

  - injects the blocker summary and next step into record and next-action
    prompts
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-result-evidence-blocker`
  - adds blocked and ready visual states
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 88 iterations

## Iteration 89 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Detail Gate

### Baseline

Iteration 88 made evidence blockers visible, but the readiness checks were still
mostly keyword based. A learner could type only labels such as `변경 파일`,
`실행 명령`, `실패/위험`, and `직접 확인` without real detail and risk opening
the acceptance gate. That conflicts with the app's purpose: vibe-coding learners
must verify AI output with concrete evidence, not empty labels.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result evidence detail gate`

The desktop evidence readiness logic now reads the evidence note line by line
and requires each dimension to include a matching detail. Changed files need a
path or filename, commands need a command plus result, failure/risk needs a
real status such as `없음`, `FAIL`, `오류`, or `통과`, and direct confirmation
needs an observed action/tool/screen detail such as `Playwright`, `스크린샷`, or
`클릭`. For example, `실패/위험 없음` and `직접 확인 Playwright` can pass when
paired with the other concrete evidence lines, while 라벨만 있는 근거 should
remain blocked.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `evidenceLineHasDetail`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceDetailLines`
  - strengthens changed-file, command, failure/risk, and direct-check readiness
    patterns so label-only evidence no longer passes
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 89 iterations

  `라벨만 있는 근거`; added those examples to this iteration note.
- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `iterations: 89`, `allPassed: true`

## Iteration 90 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Writing Guidance

### Baseline

Iteration 89 correctly blocked label-only evidence, but the evidence textarea
did not explain the rule before the learner typed. A vibe-coding learner should
not have to infer why `변경 파일`, `실행 명령`, `실패/위험`, and `직접 확인`
alone are rejected. The app needs to teach the review habit directly: source
and AI output are accepted only when the learner can name concrete files,
commands, risk status, and observed checks.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result evidence writing guidance`

The desktop implementation result evidence area now shows `AI 구현 결과 근거
작성 규칙` above the textarea. It states that labels alone do not pass, changed
files need real paths or filenames, commands need the command plus a PASS/FAIL
style result, failure/risk needs a clear status, and direct confirmation needs
an observed action such as Playwright, screenshot, click, or copy.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceWritingRules`
  - renders the rules as `AI 구현 결과 근거 작성 규칙` in the evidence input
    area
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-result-evidence-rules`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 90 iterations

## Iteration 91 - Desktop Source Purpose Contract

### Baseline

The desktop app already treated source as temporary evidence and added review
gates for AI output, but the first screen still relied on scattered copy to
explain why a GitHub URL or source folder is needed. The product goal is not to
teach traditional programming or embed source as permanent AI knowledge. It is
to help a vibe-coding learner extract project-specific purpose, architecture,
roles, terms, prompt context, and verification criteria.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source purpose contract`

The desktop first screen now shows a `소스 입력 목적 계약` panel with three
explicit commitments: source is not knowledge embedding, the learner is the AI
director, and absorbed source snapshots should be considered for cleanup only
after learning artifacts and verification records exist.

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `sourcePurposeContractItems`
  - renders `소스 입력 목적 계약` after the source status strip
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.source-purpose-contract`
  - adds responsive collapse with the existing desktop layout breakpoint
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 91 iterations

## Iteration 92 - Desktop Vibe Coding Learning Boundary

### Baseline

Iteration 91 clarified why source is accepted, but the first screen still did
not explicitly separate what the learner should not study from what the learner
must study. The user goal is a vibe-coding education app: code generation can
be delegated to AI, while the learner develops judgment about purpose,
architecture, roles, terms, prompts, and verification.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop vibe coding learning boundary`

The desktop first screen now shows `바이브코딩 학습 경계` with three cards:
`배우지 않는 것`, `반드시 배우는 것`, and `AI에게 맡기는 것`. It makes clear
that language syntax memorization and line-by-line coding are out of scope,
while architecture reasons, role boundaries, key terms, AI prompts, acceptance
criteria, and verification habits are in scope.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `learningBoundaryItems`
  - renders `바이브코딩 학습 경계` after the source purpose contract
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.learning-boundary-strip`
  - adds responsive collapse with the existing desktop layout breakpoint
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 92 iterations

cleanup record explicitly says `source/` was temporary project evidence, not

## Iteration 110 - Source Prune Applied Retained Learning Artifacts

### Baseline

Iteration 109 improved the reader-facing `SOURCE-PRUNED.md` tombstone, but
`analysis/source-prune-applied.json` remained mostly an operational deletion
record. That made the durable machine-readable record weaker than the Markdown:
tools could see what was removed, but not which learner artifacts replaced the
raw source snapshot.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune applied retained learning artifacts`

`analysis/source-prune-applied.json` now carries `sourceKnowledgePolicy` and
`retainedLearningArtifacts`. The policy records that `source/` was temporary
project evidence, not embedded AI development knowledge. The retained artifacts
come from the preserved evidence bundle so downstream tools can recover the
learner-facing purpose, prompt, implementation brief, verification summary, and
retention decision without reopening raw source.
