- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - adds `sourceKnowledgePolicy` and `retainedLearningArtifacts` to
    `SourcePruneApplyResult`
  - writes the preserved evidence bundle into the apply report
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies the apply result and JSON report contain the retained learning
    artifacts
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 110 iterations

## Iteration 109 - Source Pruned Tombstone Learner Assets

### Baseline

Iteration 108 made the dry-run prune plan more learner-facing, but the actual
`SOURCE-PRUNED.md` tombstone still mostly explained the deletion in English.
After a cleanup is applied, that tombstone becomes the durable reader-facing
record. It therefore needs to preserve the same vibe-coding learning boundary:
source was temporary evidence, not embedded AI development knowledge.

### Candidate Adopted

`source pruned tombstone learner assets`

`SOURCE-PRUNED.md` now includes `## 남긴 학습 자산` and `## 다시 소스가 필요한
경우`. The tombstone records that the original `source/` was temporary evidence
for project purpose, architecture reasons, role terms, AI prompts, and
verification criteria. It lists the retained artifacts that replace raw source
for learner review, then says to re-import source only when the retained
artifacts cannot explain a new feature's architecture reason, role boundary,
verification command, or human judgment criteria.

## Iteration 108 - Source Prune Learner Cleanup Decision

### Baseline

Iteration 107 added a dedicated preserved evidence bundle gate, but the prune
Markdown still explained the most important source-as-evidence rule in English.
That was weaker than the rest of RepoTutor's learner-facing Korean guidance.

The risk is practical: a vibe-coding learner could see `READY` and treat source
cleanup as a storage-only operation. The app should instead ask whether the
learner can still direct AI after source links stop opening.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune learner cleanup decision`

`source-prune-plan.md` now includes `## 학습자 정리 판단`. The section states
that raw `source/` is not embedded AI development knowledge, but temporary
evidence for this specific project's purpose, architecture reasons, role terms,
AI prompts, and verification criteria. It then asks cleanup-before questions
about daily summary or implementation brief, source evidence plus acceptance
criteria, session verification, preserved evidence bundle, and whether the
learner can still instruct and review a similar app after source links stop
opening.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - adds the Korean learner cleanup decision section to prune Markdown
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies the generated prune Markdown contains the learner cleanup decision
    guidance
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 108 iterations

## Iteration 107 - Source Prune Preserved Evidence Bundle Gate

### Baseline

Iteration 106 made the source absorption ledger list a concrete preserved
evidence bundle, but the `prune-source` plan still treated it as part of the
larger preserved artifact list. That was too implicit for the product goal.

For a vibe-coding learner, the question is not whether AI needs source code as
general development knowledge. AI already has broad development knowledge. The
source snapshot is useful only as temporary evidence for this specific project:
learning purpose, architecture reasons, AI prompts, verification boundaries,
and the retention decision.

### Candidate Adopted

`source prune preserved evidence bundle gate`

`prune-source` now reports and gates a dedicated preserved evidence bundle
before source cleanup can be considered. The bundle includes:
`reference/source-absorption-ledger.html`,
`analysis/daily-summary-report.json`, `html/vibe-coding-prompt-pack.html`,
`reference/vibe-coding-implementation-brief.html`,
`html/session-verification.html`, and `reference/source-retention-guide.html`.

- `packages/core/src/source-prune.ts`
  - adds `PRESERVED_EVIDENCE_BUNDLE_ARTIFACTS`
  - adds `preservedEvidenceBundleOk` and `preservedEvidenceBundle` to the prune
    plan
  - blocks cleanup with `missing-preserved-evidence-bundle:<path>` when one of
    the durable learning artifacts is missing
  - adds `## Preserved Evidence Bundle` to the Markdown plan
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - verifies the new prune-plan check and the exact preserved bundle artifacts

## Iteration 105 - Prompt A/B Lab Send Decision

### Baseline

The AI prompt A/B lab already compared a vague prompt A with a source-grounded
prompt B. The weakness was that the page did not separately state what to do
after the comparison: block A, treat B only as a READY candidate, revise B if
it does not match the learner's actual product, or stop and return to readiness
checks if both prompts are weak.

For a vibe-coding learner, the point is not to admire a better prompt. The
point is to decide whether a prompt is safe to send to AI without letting the
model fill gaps with generic development assumptions.

### Candidate Adopted

`prompt ab lab send decision`

Generated `reference/ai-prompt-ab-lab.html` and `.md` now include `보낼
프롬프트 판정`. The section marks prompt A as BLOCK, marks prompt B as a READY
candidate only after checking the learner's product purpose and decision
checkpoints, requires REVISE if B misses the new app's user/domain/exclusion
boundary, and sends the learner back to the readiness stop conditions if both
prompts are insufficient.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - adds reusable send-decision rows for the AI prompt A/B lab
  - renders the send decision in both HTML and Markdown
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the generated A/B lab contains the send decision in HTML and
    Markdown
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 105 iterations

## Iteration 104 - Prompt Readiness Send Stop Conditions

### Baseline

The AI prompt readiness checklist already scored prompt quality with READY,
REVISE, and BLOCK rows. The remaining weakness was that stop conditions were
distributed across the rows instead of being a visible top-level gate before a
learner sends a prompt to AI.

For a vibe-coding learner, this is important because the risk is not missing
syntax knowledge. The risk is sending a vague prompt that lets AI use general
development knowledge without the source-derived user problem, source evidence,
first slice, acceptance criteria, verification assertion, and failure-report
format.

### Candidate Adopted

`prompt readiness send stop conditions`

Generated `reference/ai-prompt-readiness-checklist.html` and `.md` now include
`프롬프트 전송 중단 조건`. The section blocks sending prompts that lack the
user problem and exclusion boundary, source evidence, first vertical slice plus
non-goals, acceptance criteria, verification assertions, or the required AI
reporting format for changed files, commands, failures, assumptions, and next
questions.

## Iteration 103 - Implementation Brief Learner Decision Checkpoints

### Baseline

The implementation brief already compressed purpose, architecture, first
vertical slice, acceptance criteria, verification plan, terms, non-goals, and a
copyable implementation prompt. The remaining weakness was that learner-owned
decisions were embedded indirectly inside the prompt instead of being a visible
checkpoint.

For a vibe-coding learner, this matters because AI can write code and apply
syntax, but the learner must still direct product judgment: whose problem the
app solves, which source responsibilities transfer, which acceptance criteria
are enough, what evidence counts as complete, and whether the source snapshot
can be pruned after absorption and verification.

### Candidate Adopted

`implementation brief learner decision checkpoints`

Generated `reference/vibe-coding-implementation-brief.html` and `.md` now
include `학습자 판단 체크포인트`. The section separates decisions the learner
must own from work AI can do: product/user problem judgment, keep/drop
responsibility choices, PASS / REVISE / BLOCK acceptance review, completion
evidence selection across command/test/screen/human judgment, and source
snapshot retention after checking source absorption, session verification, and
source retention artifacts.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - adds learner decision checkpoints to implementation brief data
  - renders them in both implementation brief HTML and Markdown
  - includes the checkpoints in the copyable implementation prompt

  - asserts the generated implementation brief HTML and Markdown contain the
    learner-owned decision checkpoints

## Iteration 102 - Glossary Vibe Coding Term Rules

### Baseline

The generated glossary already said that terms are not for memorization, but
the page still began directly with term cards. It did not yet teach the learner
how to use terms as vibe-coding control handles: responsibility labels, data
flow labels, verification labels, prompt wording, and review checklist items.

For this product, that is a concrete gap because the learner does not need to
become a traditional syntax-first developer. They need enough terminology to
tell AI what to build, why the structure exists, and how to check whether the
AI output preserved the source-derived intent.

### Candidate Adopted

`glossary vibe coding term rules`

Generated `reference/glossary.html` now includes `용어 사용 규칙`. The section
says terms are not developer exam words; they are responsibility labels for
communicating with AI. It tells the learner to connect each term to
responsibility, data flow, and verification criteria; to turn unknown terms
into "why is this structure needed?" questions instead of syntax memorization;
and to convert source-evidence terms into implementation prompt wording or AI
output review checklist items.

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - adds the term usage rules section to the generated glossary reference

  - asserts the generated glossary contains the term usage rules and
    vibe-coding term-to-prompt framing

  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 102 iterations

## Iteration 101 - Rebuild Cheatsheet Vibe Coding Rules

### Baseline

The generated rebuild cheatsheet already gave a vertical-slice order, but it
could still read like a traditional implementation checklist. It did not yet
state the vibe-coding split directly: the learner owns purpose, user problem,
architecture responsibility, acceptance criteria, and verification questions;
AI owns code writing, repeated implementation, and syntax details.

For this product, that distinction matters because raw GitHub/source folders
are temporary evidence. RepoTutor should convert source evidence into AI
implementation instructions and review criteria, not copy full source into app
knowledge or teach line-by-line coding.

### Candidate Adopted

`rebuild cheatsheet vibe coding rules`

Generated `reference/rebuild-cheatsheet.html` now includes `바이브코딩 재구현
규칙`. The section says the learner should first fix product purpose, user
problem, architecture responsibility, acceptance criteria, and verification
questions; AI should handle code writing, repeated implementation, and syntax
details; source evidence should become implementation instructions and review
criteria instead of full-source copying or embedded app knowledge; and each
slice needs an explicit verification method.

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - adds the vibe-coding rebuild rules section to the generated rebuild
    cheatsheet

  - asserts the generated rebuild cheatsheet contains the learner/AI split,
    no-full-source-copy rule, source-evidence conversion rule, and verification
    method reminder

## Iteration 95 - Teaching Mission Learning Boundary

### Baseline

The desktop first screen, generated start report, and generated README already
said that RepoTutor is not a traditional syntax course. The generated
`MISSION.md` also explained that source is not embedded AI knowledge, but it did
not yet carry the same explicit three-part learner boundary. That made the root
mission weaker than the rest of the session.

### Candidate Adopted

`teaching mission learning boundary`

Generated `MISSION.md` now includes `학습 경계` with three rules: what not to
learn, what must be learned, and what AI should handle. This keeps the session
mission aligned with vibe-coding learners who need product purpose,
architecture reasons, role boundaries, key terms, prompts, acceptance criteria,
and verification habits rather than line-by-line implementation practice.

## Iteration 94 - Study README Learning Boundary

### Baseline

Iteration 93 moved the learning boundary into `vibe-coding-start.md/html`, but
the root `README.study.md` still opened with Start Here and Source Evidence
Policy only. A learner browsing the generated session folder could read the
README before any HTML page, so the root artifact should also state the
non-goals and responsibilities.

### Candidate Adopted

`study readme learning boundary`

Generated `README.study.md` now includes `Vibe-Coding Learning Boundary` with
three rules: syntax memorization and line-by-line coding are not the goal,
purpose/architecture/roles/terms/prompts/acceptance/verification are the
learning target, and actual code generation is delegated to AI.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds the boundary section to `readmeStudy`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the generated README includes each boundary rule
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 94 iterations

## Iteration 84 - Desktop Implementation Result Evidence Note

### Baseline

Iteration 83 turned each result verdict into a next-action prompt, but the
verdict could still be checked without recording what AI actually changed,
which commands ran, which failures remained, or what the learner observed on
screen. A vibe-coding learner needs a small evidence note so the review is tied
to real implementation evidence instead of empty confidence.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result evidence note`

The desktop implementation result review panel now includes a `구현 결과 근거
메모` textarea. The note is included in the review, revision, record, and
next-action prompts. Empty notes explicitly tell the learner to capture changed
files, commands, failure logs, and manually observed screens before accepting or
revising the result.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `implementationResultEvidenceText`
  - adds `normalizedImplementationResultEvidence`
  - renders `AI 구현 결과 근거 메모`
  - injects the evidence note into result review, revision, record, and
    next-action prompts
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-result-evidence` textarea styling
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 84 iterations

## Iteration 83 - Desktop Implementation Result Next Action Prompt

### Baseline

Iteration 82 made the current implementation result verdict visible, but the
learner still had to decide which long prompt to copy. A vibe-coding learner
needs the app to turn `BLOCK`, `REVISE`, or `ACCEPT` into the immediate AI
instruction: stop and gather missing context, revise the same first slice, or
record the accepted result and propose only one next small slice.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result next action prompt`

The desktop implementation result review panel now generates a copyable `결과
다음 행동 프롬프트`. It is driven by the same checklist-derived decision
summary. `BLOCK 검토` asks AI not to implement yet and to ask missing-context
questions; `REVISE 검토` blocks moving to the next feature and scopes the fix
inside the first vertical slice; `ACCEPT 후보` records the result and asks for
one next small improvement without embedding more source or rewriting the app.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `implementationResultNextActionPrompt`
  - adds `copyImplementationResultNextActionPrompt`
  - renders `AI 구현 결과 다음 행동 프롬프트`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-result-next-action` prompt-block styles and mobile
    one-column behavior
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 83 iterations

## Iteration 82 - Desktop Implementation Result Decision Summary

### Baseline

Iteration 81 could generate a record prompt, but the practical judgment was
still easiest to see inside the long prompt text. A vibe-coding learner needs a
visible decision strip that says whether to stop, revise, or accept the AI
implementation result before copying any record.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result decision summary`

The desktop implementation result review panel now shows an `AI 구현 결과 판정
요약` strip. It derives the decision from the existing review checklist and
shows the current verdict, completed count, incomplete count, and next action:
stop and fill missing criteria, revise within the first vertical slice, or
record the result and move to the next small improvement.

  - adds responsive `.implementation-result-decision` styles

## Iteration 80 - Desktop Implementation Result Review Checklist

### Baseline

Iteration 79 added a result review prompt, but the learner still had no visible
state for which review criteria they had actually checked. A vibe-coding
learner should not have to read code line by line; they need to mark whether
purpose, scope, role boundaries, verification evidence, and learner-level
understanding are good enough to accept the AI result.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result review checklist`

The desktop implementation result review panel now includes a checklist for
purpose preservation, scope limit, role boundary, verification evidence, and
learner judgment. It shows whether the result is an `ACCEPT 후보` or still
needs `REVISE/BLOCK` review, and generates a copyable `결과 재작업 프롬프트`
from unchecked criteria.

## Iteration 79 - Desktop Implementation Result Review Prompt

### Baseline

Iteration 78 helped the learner repair missing handoff context before
implementation, but the desktop still lacked a post-implementation review
prompt. In vibe coding, AI can write the code, but the learner must still judge
whether the result preserves purpose, stays inside the first vertical slice,
keeps role boundaries, and proves behavior through verification.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation result review prompt`

The desktop implementation handoff panel now includes a visible `구현 결과 검토
프롬프트`. It asks AI to review the generated first vertical slice against
purpose preservation, scope limit, file/role boundary, verification evidence,
and learner judgment. The output is framed as `ACCEPT / REVISE / BLOCK` plus a
follow-up correction prompt, not a line-by-line syntax lecture.

## Iteration 78 - Desktop Implementation Handoff Repair Prompt

### Baseline

Iteration 77 showed whether the implementation handoff was ready, but it still
left the learner to decide how to fix missing context. For this product, the
learner is not trying to become a traditional syntax-first developer. When
readiness is incomplete, the app should help them ask AI for the missing
purpose, role, verification, or source-boundary context before any code is
generated.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation handoff repair prompt`

The desktop implementation handoff panel now includes a visible `맥락 보강
프롬프트`. It turns missing readiness items into an AI prompt that asks for
learner questions, brief sentences to add, reports to inspect, implementation
hold conditions, and a later first vertical slice prompt. The prompt explicitly
tells AI not to start implementation yet.

  - adds `implementationHandoffMissingChecks`
  - adds `implementationHandoffRepairPrompt`

  - renders `AI 구현 인계 맥락 보강 프롬프트`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-handoff-repair` styles
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 78 iterations

## Iteration 77 - Desktop Implementation Handoff Readiness

### Baseline

Iteration 76 added a copyable implementation handoff prompt, but it did not
show whether the learner had enough context to send that prompt immediately.
For a vibe-coding learner, the important question is not whether they know code
syntax. It is whether they have a session artifact, learner goal, verification
standard, and source boundary ready before asking AI to build the first slice.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation handoff readiness`

The desktop implementation handoff panel now shows `AI 구현 인계 준비도`.
It checks session artifacts, learner goal readiness, session verification, and
source boundary visibility. The generated handoff prompt also includes the
readiness summary so AI can ask for missing context instead of guessing.

## Iteration 76 - Desktop Implementation Handoff Prompt

### Baseline

Iterations 74 and 75 made source cleanup decisions visible and copyable, but
the main session screen still did not give the learner one obvious post-analysis
handoff prompt for asking AI to implement a similar app. The reports existed,
but a vibe-coding learner should not have to manually gather the implementation
brief, prompt readiness checklist, prompt pack, and verification report before
asking AI for the first vertical slice.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop implementation handoff prompt`

The desktop session view now renders an `AI 구현 인계 프롬프트` panel. It
builds a copyable prompt from the current session path, learner goal, core
implementation reports, and guardrails that source is temporary project
evidence rather than permanent AI knowledge. The prompt tells AI to avoid a
syntax lecture, cut the work to one first vertical slice, and return file/role
mapping, acceptance criteria, verification commands, human review checks, and
the next question.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `implementationHandoffCheckpoints`
  - adds `implementationHandoffPrompt`
  - adds `readableReportPath` so reference report paths do not render as
    `/html/../reference/...`
  - adds `copyImplementationHandoffPrompt`
  - renders `AI 구현 인계 프롬프트` with quick links to 구현 브리프,
    프롬프트 준비도, 프롬프트 팩, and 검증
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds `.implementation-handoff` and link button styles
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`

## Iteration 75 - Desktop Source Retention Decision Prompt Preview

### Baseline

Iteration 74 made the source-retention decision prompt copyable, but the
learner could not inspect the generated prompt before copying it. That matters
for a vibe-coding learner because the app should make the AI handoff visible:
what source status, absorbed artifacts, blockers, and cleanup boundary will be
sent to AI for the `READY / HOLD / PRUNED` decision.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source retention decision prompt preview`

The desktop retention panel now renders a visible prompt preview before the
copy action. This keeps the source snapshot as temporary evidence and lets the
learner review the exact architecture, evidence, blocker, verification, and
cleanup-boundary context that will be handed to AI.

## Iteration 74 - Desktop Source Retention Decision Prompt Copy

### Baseline

Iteration 73 made source retention recommendations Korean-first, but the
learner still had to manually rewrite the current session status when asking AI
to re-check whether cleanup was safe. That is friction in a vibe-coding workflow
where the learner should be able to hand AI precise context and ask for a
guarded decision.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source retention decision prompt copy`

The desktop retention panel now builds a copyable source-retention decision
prompt from the current session path, source state, absorbed artifact coverage,
additional-investigation verdict, verification status, blockers, and Korean
recommendation. The prompt explicitly asks for `READY / HOLD / PRUNED` and
states that cleanup may target only the generated session `source/` snapshot,
not the original user source.

## Iteration 70 - Desktop Source Absorption Summary

### Baseline

The desktop retention panel showed whether a generated `source/` snapshot was
blocked, ready, or already pruned. It also linked to the absorption ledger, but
the learner still had to open another page to see the key decision: what was
absorbed into durable learning artifacts, whether more investigation was still
needed, and whether cleanup could be considered.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source absorption summary`

The retention panel now renders a `소스 흡수 요약` section derived from the same
`source_prune_plan` data used by the CLI cleanup gate. It shows preserved
artifact coverage, an additional-investigation verdict, and whether session
verification has passed. This keeps the user's rule visible: source cleanup is
reasonable only after the app has preserved the absorbed learning outputs and
there is no unresolved verification or investigation blocker.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - extends `SourcePruneResponse` with `checks` and `preservedArtifacts`
  - derives `preservedArtifactCount`, `preservedArtifactTotal`,
    `sourceAbsorptionVerdict`, and `sourceAbsorptionEvidence`
  - renders `소스 흡수 요약` inside the retention panel
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - styles `absorption-summary`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 70 iterations

## Iteration 69 - Desktop Vibe Coding Intent Guard

### Baseline

The desktop app already said that source is temporary evidence, not permanent
embedded product knowledge. The remaining risk was that the copyable prompts
could still let an AI answer like a traditional programming tutor, focusing on
syntax or line-by-line coding rather than helping a vibe-coding learner direct
and review AI.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop vibe coding intent guard`

The learner prompt draft and the AI response revision prompt now explicitly
state that AI already has general development knowledge, source should be used
only as project-specific evidence, and the teaching goal is not a traditional
syntax lesson. The prompt asks for purpose, responsibility boundaries,
essential terms, architecture rationale, acceptance criteria, and verification
steps that help the learner brief AI toward a similar app.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - extends the learner brief scaffold with the learner's vibe-coding study
    mode
  - adds the source-as-evidence and non-traditional-coding guard to the
    copyable implementation prompt
  - adds the same guard to the AI response revision prompt
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 69 iterations

## Iteration 67 - Desktop AI Response Revision Prompt

### Baseline

Iteration 66 made the AI response review checklist interactive, but a learner who
left one or more review criteria unchecked still needed to invent a follow-up
prompt manually. That is a weak handoff for vibe-coding learners because the
app should turn review gaps into precise AI revision instructions.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop ai response revision prompt`

The command area now derives `aiResponseRevisionMissingLabels` and renders an
`AI 응답 보강 프롬프트 초안`. When any review criterion is unchecked, the draft
names the missing criteria and asks AI to preserve the learner goal, separate
architecture/folder/file responsibilities, and split static evidence from
runtime/test verification needs. When all criteria are checked, it switches to a
completion message that tells the learner to execute the first vertical slice
and compare results against the acceptance criteria.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `aiResponseRevisionMissingLabels`
  - adds `aiResponseRevisionPromptLines`
  - adds `aiResponseRevisionPrompt`
  - renders `AI 응답 보강 프롬프트 초안`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - styles `ai-response-revision`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 67 iterations

## Iteration 66 - Desktop AI Response Review State

### Baseline

Iteration 65 showed the AI response review criteria, but they were still static
chips. A vibe-coding learner could read the criteria, but could not mark that
an AI answer had been checked for product intent, role boundaries, and
verification evidence.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop ai response review state`

The command area now tracks `aiResponseReviewState` for the review criteria,
shows an `AI 응답 검토 0/3 완료` style summary, lets the learner check each
criterion, logs each completion or release, and offers `검토 초기화`. This turns
the review rubric into a small operational loop for judging AI output instead
of studying syntax.

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `aiResponseReviewState`
  - adds `aiResponseReviewDoneCount` and `aiResponseReviewSummary`
  - adds `toggleAiResponseReview`
  - adds `resetAiResponseReview`
  - adds `prependLogOnce` and `visibleLog` to keep StrictMode duplicate log entries from rendering twice
  - renders the review criteria as checkboxes with a reset action
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - styles checked review labels, the review summary, and `ai-response-review-reset`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 66 iterations

## Iteration 65 - Desktop AI Response Review Checklist

### Baseline

Iteration 64 made it easy to copy the AI implementation instruction, but after
the learner pastes that prompt into AI, the desktop app still did not show what
to inspect in the AI response. That matters for a vibe-coding learner because
the goal is to review AI architecture and verification judgment, not to memorize
syntax.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop ai response review checklist`

The command area now renders `AI 응답 검토 기준` with three review checks:
`목적 보존`, `역할 경계`, and `검증 근거`. These remind the learner to confirm
that the AI response preserves the original goal, separates architecture/folder/
file responsibilities, and distinguishes static evidence from runtime/test
verification needs.

## Iteration 64 - Desktop Learner Brief Prompt Copy

### Baseline

Iteration 63 showed an `AI 구현 지시문 초안`, but the learner still had to
manually select and copy the prompt before moving it into an AI chat. That was a
workflow gap for vibe-coding learners who need to transfer context quickly
rather than edit source code by hand.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop learner brief prompt copy`

The prompt draft panel now includes an `AI 지시문 복사` action. The action writes
the generated prompt to `navigator.clipboard` when available, falls back to a
temporary textarea copy path when needed, and records a progress-log line that
tells the learner the first vertical slice instruction is ready to paste into AI.

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - imports the `Copy` icon
  - adds `copyLearnerPromptDraft`
  - renders the `AI 지시문 복사` button beside the prompt draft title

  - styles `prompt-draft-header` and `prompt-copy-button`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 64 iterations

## Iteration 63 - Desktop Learner Brief Prompt Draft

### Baseline

Iteration 62 told the learner which context to add next, but the desktop app
still did not show how that context becomes an AI implementation instruction.
That left a gap between "the brief is ready" and "ask AI to build the first
similar feature."

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop learner brief prompt draft`

The command area now derives `learnerPromptDraftLines` and renders an
`AI 구현 지시문 초안` preview under the learner brief. The draft uses the
current source target, the learner's brief, a first `vertical slice` request,
and explicit verification instructions. This reinforces that the learner's job
is to provide product intent, architecture roles, file responsibility context,
and acceptance criteria to AI rather than memorize syntax.

## Iteration 62 - Desktop Learner Brief Next Step

### Baseline

Iteration 61 summarized readiness as `브리프 준비도 N/3`, but a vibe-coding
learner still had to infer which missing context should be added before asking
AI to implement similar software.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop learner brief next step`

The readiness area now derives `learnerBriefMissingLabels` and renders
`learnerBriefNextStep`. When context is missing, it says `다음 보강:` and names
the missing purpose/problem, structure/role, or verification/AI instruction
context that should be added as one sentence. When all three are ready, it says
`준비 완료. 학습 시작 후 AI 구현 브리프와 검증 기준을 확인하세요`. This keeps
the learner focused on AI instruction context instead of traditional syntax
study.

## Iteration 60 - Desktop Learner Brief Scaffold

### Baseline

Iteration 59 showed whether the learner brief had enough purpose, structure,
and verification context, but it still left non-traditional developers to
invent the missing wording from scratch. That is a small usability gap for the
intended vibe-coding workflow, where the learner should shape AI direction
rather than memorize development vocabulary.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop learner brief scaffold`

The learner brief readiness area now includes `브리프 예시 추가`. It inserts a
short editable scaffold for goal, architecture role learning, AI instruction,
and verification criteria. The scaffold is intentionally about AI direction and
review context, not line-by-line syntax study.

    scaffold without overwriting existing learner text
  - logs that the brief was scaffolded

  - styles `brief-scaffold-button` inside the readiness chip group

  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 60 iterations

## Iteration 59 - Desktop Learner Brief Readiness Cues

### Baseline

The desktop app accepts a learner's goal, PRD, issue, or AI prompt, but the
textarea did not give immediate feedback on whether the learner had supplied
the kind of context a vibe-coding learner needs. A broad request could still
look ready even when it did not mention purpose, structure, responsibility, AI
handoff, or verification criteria.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop learner brief readiness cues`

The learner brief input now shows three lightweight readiness cues: purpose or
problem, structure or role, and verification or AI instruction. The cues make
the product stance explicit: the learner does not need a traditional syntax
drill, but should give AI enough context to build, review, and verify a similar
system.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `normalizedLearnerBrief`
  - adds `learnerBriefReadinessChecks`
  - renders `바이브코딩 브리프 준비도` under the learner brief textarea
  - marks each cue as `준비됨` or `보강 필요`
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - styles the readiness cues as compact status chips
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 59 iterations

## Iteration 58 - Desktop Quiz Answer Reset

### Baseline

The desktop quiz can now focus and filter missing AI instruction-context
judgments, but learners could not clear their current answers without reloading
the quiz. That made a full retry awkward after experimenting with answers or
after reviewing a submitted attempt.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop quiz answer reset`

The quiz header now includes `답변 초기화`. It clears selected answers, removes
the latest attempt result, exits the missing-only filter, and logs that the
learner is restarting the AI instruction-context judgment practice.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `resetQuizAnswers`
  - clears answers, attempt state, and missing-only filter state
  - disables reset until there is an answer or submitted attempt to clear
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 58 iterations

## Iteration 57 - Desktop Quiz Missing Only Filter

### Baseline

The quiz header now shows missing question numbers and shortcut buttons, but
the full quiz list still stays visible. On longer quizzes, completed questions
can still distract the learner from the remaining AI instruction-context
judgments.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop quiz missing only filter`

The quiz header now includes a `미완료만 보기` / `전체 보기` toggle. When active,
the list shows only unanswered questions while preserving the original question
numbers. When no questions remain, the filtered view shows a completion empty
state.

  - adds `showOnlyMissingQuizQuestions`
  - derives `visibleQuizQuestions`
  - renders `quiz-filter-summary`
  - adds the missing-only toggle with `aria-pressed`
  - renders `quiz-filter-empty` when the filtered list is complete
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - styles the filter summary and empty state
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 57 iterations

## Iteration 54 - Desktop Quiz First Missing Navigation

### Baseline

The desktop quiz now shows which questions are missing answers, but a long quiz
still requires manual scanning to find the next unfinished AI
instruction-context judgment. That adds friction for vibe-coding learners who
need to focus on purpose, responsibility, and verification choices instead of
UI hunting.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop quiz first missing navigation`

The quiz header now includes `미완료 문항 찾기`. While any answer is missing, the
button scrolls to the first missing question, focuses its first choice, and logs
how many AI instruction-context judgments remain.

  - adds `focusFirstMissingQuizQuestion`

## Iteration 53 - Desktop Quiz Answer Accessibility State

### Baseline

Iteration 52 made each quiz card show `선택 필요` or `답변 완료`, but the selected
choice itself was still exposed mainly through CSS. That makes the UI readable
to sighted users, but weaker for keyboard/screen-reader workflows and automated
state checks.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop quiz answer accessibility state`

Quiz answer buttons now expose their selected state with `aria-pressed`, and
question cards expose their completion state through an `aria-label`. The state
badge also uses `aria-live` so answer-state changes are announced as the learner
turns source understanding into AI instruction-context judgments.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - derives `selectedAnswer` per question
  - adds question-card `aria-label` for `답변 완료` / `선택 필요`
  - adds `aria-live="polite"` to the state badge
  - adds `aria-pressed` and a selected-state `aria-label` to each choice button
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 53 iterations

- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `iterations: 53`, `allPassed: true`
- Playwright desktop UI smoke against Vite `http://127.0.0.1:1420/`: PASS
  - mocked Tauri bridge loaded two quiz questions
  - confirmed initial question `aria-label` values for `선택 필요`
  - selected answers and confirmed two `aria-pressed="true"` choices

## Iteration 52 - Desktop Quiz Per Question Answer State

### Baseline

The desktop quiz now blocks submission until every answer is selected, but the
question cards did not show which specific AI instruction-context judgments
were still missing. With multiple questions, the learner had to scan the choices

transition from missing answer to answered question.

## Iteration 50 - Desktop Quiz Learning Record Log

### Baseline

The quiz result panel now explains the learning record, but the progress log
still only said `퀴즈 제출 완료`. After the learner navigates to another report,
that log line did not preserve the record path or the next action.

`desktop quiz learning record log`

Quiz submission now adds separate log lines for the learning record path and the
review action. Wrong answers tell the learner to rewrite missed concepts as AI
instruction context with purpose, responsibility, and verification criteria.
Passing attempts tell the learner to confirm the passing evidence in the
learning record.

## Iteration 48 - Source As Learner Briefing Evidence

### Baseline

The app already said source snapshots are temporary evidence, but the user
clarified a sharper product boundary: AI already has general development
knowledge, and the learner is a vibe-coding developer rather than a traditional
line-by-line programmer. Therefore the app must not imply that source is being
embedded to teach AI how to code, or that the learner must study vocabulary for
a traditional coding exam.

### Candidate Adopted

`source as learner briefing evidence`

The product copy and generated teaching artifacts now state that source intake
is a learner briefing process. RepoTutor uses source to extract project intent,
responsibility maps, architecture reasons, AI instruction context, and
verification criteria so the learner can brief, steer, and review AI.

### App Changes

- `README.md`
  - adds a source-intake contract: source is not AI training data; it is a
    learner briefing process.
- `docs/product/learning-mission.md`
  - clarifies that AI already knows common coding patterns and source removes
    ambiguity for the specific project.
  - clarifies that terms are for AI steering and review, not a traditional
    coding exam.
- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - generated `MISSION.md` now says source is not embedded data for teaching AI
    development knowledge.
  - generated learner role contract now says source is evidence for extracting
    project context to give AI.
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the generated mission and learner role contract contain the new
    source-as-briefing boundary.
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 48 iterations.

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `git diff --check`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS

  PASS; generated `MISSION.md`, `learner-role-contract.html`, and
  `learner-role-contract.md` contain the source-as-briefing boundary
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core build`: PASS

## Iteration 40 - Vibe Tech Context Language Report

### Baseline

The shared target description for `language` already said the page is not about
syntax memorization. The actual generated `language.md` and `language.html`
were weaker: they listed the primary language and tradeoffs, which could still
look like a traditional programming-language lesson.

### Candidate Adopted

`vibe tech context language report`

The generated language/tech-stack report now says that language is not the
learning goal. It frames languages as AI instruction context: runtime,
framework, build tool, file-role, and verification information the learner can
give to an AI agent.

    criteria

  - asserts generated `language.html` and `language.md` include the new
    vibe-coding language-context guidance

## Iteration 44 - Quiz Print Wrong Note AI Review

### Baseline

Iteration 43 changed generated quiz questions into AI instruction-context
practice, but related surfaces still sounded generic. `quiz-print.html` was an
ordinary answer key, `wrong-notes.html` had no leading explanation of what to do
with mistakes, and the learning path still described the quiz as simple
understanding review.

### Candidate Adopted

`quiz print wrong note ai review`

The print answer key and wrong-note surfaces now explain that the quiz is not a
coding syntax worksheet. It is a way to turn source roles into AI implementation
instructions and to rewrite missed items as purpose, responsibility, and
verification criteria.

### App Changes

- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - adds `data-source-pattern="quiz-ai-instruction-review"` to quiz review
    panels
  - updates `quiz.html` intro copy around purpose, responsibility, and
    verification criteria
  - updates `quiz-print.html` to call the print sheet an AI implementation
    instruction guide rather than a syntax answer key
  - adds an `AI 지시 맥락 복습` panel to `wrong-notes.html`
  - updates the learning path quiz goal to source-role-to-AI-instruction review
- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - updates generated `wrong-notes.md` with the same AI instruction-context
    purpose
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated quiz, print, wrong-note, and learning-path surfaces expose
    the new framing
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for quiz print/wrong-note AI review and raise
    `audit:brief` to 44 iterations

## Iteration 42 - Agent Instruction Skeleton Prompt Pack

### Baseline

RepoTutor already generated a prompt pack, but the copy/paste prompt did not
show a generic AI-tool instruction skeleton. A vibe-coding learner needs a
stable frame for Copilot/Codex-style tools: role and objective first, then
project context, source evidence, work rules, and verification criteria.

### External Evidence

- GitHub Docs, "Adding repository custom instructions for GitHub Copilot":
  https://docs.github.com/copilot/customizing-copilot/adding-custom-instructions-for-github-copilot
- OpenAI API docs, "Prompt engineering":
  https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/prompt-engineering

These sources support the adopted pattern: give AI project-specific context,
build/test/validation expectations, clear instructions up front, separated
context, and a verification-oriented workflow for longer agentic tasks.

### Candidate Adopted

`agent instruction skeleton prompt pack`

The generated prompt pack now includes `AI 도구 지시서 골격`, a paste-ready
template with:

- `역할과 목표`
- `프로젝트 맥락`
- `작업 규칙`
- `검증 루브릭`

The skeleton keeps the learner's role aligned: not memorizing syntax, not
embedding raw source, but steering an AI agent with clear context and review
criteria.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds the skeleton to `vibe-coding-prompt-pack.md`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - adds a `Copilot/OpenAI agent instruction context` panel to
    `vibe-coding-prompt-pack.html`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated prompt-pack HTML and Markdown contain the skeleton
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage and raise `audit:brief` to 42 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/html build`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS

## Iteration 43 - Quiz AI Instruction Context

### Baseline

RepoTutor's generated artifacts already made the app direction explicit: a
vibe-coding learner does not need to become a traditional syntax-first
developer, and raw source should be temporary evidence rather than permanent app
knowledge. The remaining mismatch was the quiz surface. Questions still asked
for the "most fitting description" of a topic, which could feel like ordinary
recognition practice instead of training the learner to brief AI.

### Candidate Adopted

`quiz ai instruction context`

Generated quiz questions now ask how to explain a folder, file, or term to AI.
The correct answer frames the item as purpose, responsibility, and verification
criteria. Distractors explicitly reject syntax memorization, permanent source
embedding, and one-shot AI implementation without checks. Wrong notes now tell
the learner to rewrite the missed item as an AI instruction sentence.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/quiz.ts`
  - changes generated quiz question text to AI instruction context practice
  - reframes choices around purpose, responsibility, source retention, and
    verification
  - updates wrong-note review text and mini lesson for vibe-coding learners
  - updates mistake reasons toward AI prompt context and verification gaps
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated `quiz.html` and `analysis/quiz.json` include the new AI
    instruction-context wording
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for quiz AI instruction context and raise
    `audit:brief` to 43 iterations

  때 가장 알맞은 지시 맥락`, `언어 문법을 먼저 외우라고`, and `원본 소스를
  앱에 영구 내장 지식`

## Iteration 39 - Desktop Source Cleanup Checkpoints

### Baseline

The generated guides already said that source is evidence, not permanent app
knowledge. The desktop retention panel, however, still made cleanup look like a
storage operation: check status, then delete. That could confuse a vibe-coding
learner into thinking RepoTutor exists to keep source code rather than to
distill purpose, architecture, terms, prompts, and verification criteria.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop source cleanup checkpoints`

The desktop source retention panel now says that AI already has general
development knowledge and that `source/` is temporary evidence, not permanent
embedded app knowledge. It also adds a `소스 정리 전 확인` checkpoint row with
direct buttons for the three artifacts that matter before cleanup:

- `소스 흡수 기록`
- `세션 검증`
- `소스 보존 판단`

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `sourceCleanupCheckpoints`
  - adds the `검증` report tab label
  - adds `openReportTab`
  - adds `retention-purpose` copy explaining temporary source evidence
  - adds `retention-checkpoints` buttons for source absorption, verification,
    and retention guide targets
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - styles the retention purpose copy and checkpoint button row
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for desktop source cleanup checkpoints and raise
    `audit:brief` to 39 iterations

## Iteration 29 - Desktop Vibe-First Session Landing

### Baseline

The generated artifacts already contained `vibe-coding-start`,
`source-absorption-ledger`, and `source-retention-guide`, but the desktop app
still defaulted to the generic purpose tab after startup, study completion, and
historical session selection. That made the most important product stance less
obvious: RepoTutor is not trying to embed a codebase or teach line-by-line
syntax. It should first route a vibe-coding learner to the compressed guide for
purpose, architecture, terms, prompt strategy, verification, and source cleanup
judgment.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop vibe-first session landing`

The desktop app now lands on the `바이브코딩 시작` tab and
`vibe-coding-start` target by default. New studies and selected historical
sessions reset to that same target. The tutor panel now exposes direct actions
for `소스 흡수 기록` and `소스 보존 판단`, so the learner can immediately see
what the app absorbed from the source, what no longer needs investigation, and
whether the generated session source snapshot can be pruned.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `vibeCodingStartTab` and `vibeCodingStartTarget`
  - uses those constants for initial `activeTab` and `selectedTarget`
  - resets new and selected sessions to the vibe-coding start target
  - adds tutor-panel shortcuts for `바이브코딩 시작`, `소스 흡수 기록`, and
    `소스 보존 판단`
  - renames the desktop tab label from `흡수 Ledger` to `소스 흡수`
- `packages/shared/src/report-targets.ts`
  - renames the shared source absorption title to `소스 흡수 기록`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the desktop vibe-first session landing and
    raise `audit:brief` to 29 iterations

## Iteration 30 - Desktop Session Auto Refresh

### Baseline

After the desktop landing flow became vibe-first, the sidebar still required a
manual `새로고침` click before historical sessions appeared. That is a weak
resume experience for the product goal: a vibe-coding learner should be able to
open the app and immediately continue from prior GitHub/source analyses without
first remembering an operational refresh step.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop session auto refresh`

The desktop app now performs 세션 목록 자동 로드 on mount by calling
`refreshSessions({ autoResumeLatest: true })` in a React `useEffect`. The
manual refresh button remains available, but the default startup path now shows
existing session rows as soon as the app opens.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - imports `useEffect`
  - calls `void refreshSessions({ autoResumeLatest: true });` during initial
    mount
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for desktop session auto refresh and raise
    `audit:brief` to 30 iterations

## Iteration 31 - Desktop Latest Session Auto Resume

### Baseline

Iteration 30 made historical sessions appear automatically, but the learner
still had to choose a row before any report opened. Because `listSessions`
already returns sessions sorted by newest `createdAt`, the desktop app can use
the first row as the natural resume target and open the vibe-coding start guide
immediately.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop latest session auto resume`

The desktop app now performs 최신 세션 자동 이어보기 on the first successful
startup session refresh. A `didAutoResumeSession` ref prevents React StrictMode
or repeated refresh calls from repeatedly changing the active session. The
selected latest session still routes through the existing `selectSession`
helper, so it lands on `바이브코딩 시작` and refreshes source retention state.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - imports `useRef`
  - adds `didAutoResumeSession`
  - adds the `autoResumeLatest` refresh option
  - selects `result[0]` only once during startup auto refresh
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for desktop latest session auto resume and raise
    `audit:brief` to 31 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: initially FAIL because
  the manual refresh button still used `onClick={refreshSessions}` after the
  function gained an options argument; fixed with `onClick={() =>
  refreshSessions()}`
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS
- Playwright desktop UI smoke against Vite `http://localhost:1420/`: PASS

  - latest session became the current session automatically
  - active tab stayed `시작`
  - iframe opened `/tmp/repotutor-latest-session/html/vibe-coding-start.html`
  - source retention refreshed `/tmp/repotutor-latest-session`
  - screenshot: `/tmp/repotutor-desktop-latest-session-auto-resume.png`
- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `iterations: 31`, `allPassed: true`
- `pnpm verify:learning-targets`: PASS, `targets: 32`

## Iteration 35 - Vibe Start Source Purpose Primer

### Baseline

The app already had source-retention controls and a vibe-first start page, but
the first learning artifact did not directly answer the user's core product
question: if AI already has broad development knowledge, why provide source at
all? Without that answer, learners could misread source intake as permanent
knowledge embedding or as traditional syntax study.

### Candidate Adopted

`vibe start source purpose primer`

The start guide now says the source is project-specific evidence, not a
permanent knowledge payload. It explains that AI already has general
development knowledge, while the supplied GitHub/source folder anchors this
project's purpose, architecture choices, responsibility boundaries, and
verification criteria. It also states that the vibe-coding learner does not
need to memorize syntax; the learner needs to know what roles, standards, and
checks to give AI.

  - adds the same source-purpose primer to generated `vibe-coding-start.html`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts both HTML and Markdown include the source-purpose primer
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the primer and raise `audit:brief` to 35
    iterations

## Iteration 36 - Vibe Start Source Cleanup Checkpoint

### Baseline

The start guide explained why source is temporary evidence and linked to source
retention later in the flow, but it did not expose the complete cleanup
decision chain in one place. A learner could understand that source should not
be permanently embedded, yet still miss the immediate sequence: confirm what
was absorbed, verify the session artifacts, then decide whether the raw
`source/` snapshot can be pruned.

### Candidate Adopted

`vibe start source cleanup checkpoint`

The first learning page now includes `소스 정리 전 체크포인트`. It directs the
learner to the source absorption ledger, session verification report, and
source retention guide before source cleanup. This preserves the product model:
source is project-specific evidence, the learning assets are the durable output,
and cleanup should happen only after generated artifacts prove the useful
knowledge has been retained.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds `## 18. 소스 정리 전 체크포인트` to generated
    `vibe-coding-start.md`
- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - adds the same checkpoint card to generated `vibe-coding-start.html`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated HTML and Markdown include links to
    `source-absorption-ledger.html`, `session-verification.html`, and
    `source-retention-guide.html`
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`

## Iteration 37 - Study README Source Cleanup Gate

### Baseline

The HTML and Markdown start guides had a cleanup checkpoint, but a user opening
only the session-root `README.study.md` still had to infer the exact CLI safety
flow. That is weak for the user's desired cleanup model because raw source
pruning must remain explicit, dry-run-first, and tied to preserved learning
artifacts.

### Candidate Adopted

`study readme source cleanup gate`

Generated `README.study.md` now includes a `Source Cleanup Gate` section. It
shows the dry-run `prune-source <session> --format markdown` command first, then
shows the destructive command only with `--apply --confirm
DELETE-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT`. The README tells learners to apply cleanup only after
the dry-run reports READY and preserved learning artifacts are present.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/markdown.ts`
  - adds `## Source Cleanup Gate` to generated `README.study.md`
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts the generated README includes `prune-source`, dry-run markdown
    output, the explicit delete confirmation token, and the concrete session
    root
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the README cleanup gate and raise
    `audit:brief` to 37 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "generates a complete study session"`:
  PASS; generated `README.study.md` includes the dry-run and explicit apply
  commands
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core build`: PASS
- `git diff --check`: PASS
- `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS, `iterations: 37`, `allPassed: true`
- `pnpm verify:learning-targets`: PASS, `targets: 32`
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm build && pnpm audit:brief`: PASS,
  `iterations: 37`, `allPassed: true`

### Not Adopted

- No source snapshot was deleted.
- No one-click cleanup was added.
- No automatic cleanup after study generation was added.

## Iteration 38 - Source Absorption Cleanup Verdict

### Baseline

The source absorption ledger already listed absorbed artifacts and research
gaps, but the cleanup conclusion was spread across the page. The user's desired
workflow needs a clear handoff: what was absorbed, whether further analysis is
still needed, and whether raw source cleanup can be considered.

### Candidate Adopted

`source absorption cleanup verdict`

The source absorption ledger now includes `조사 종료 요약`. It counts absorbed
learning artifacts, counts remaining investigation gaps, and shows `정리 판단`
as either `정리 검토 가능` or `정리 보류`. The verdict stays conservative:
source cleanup is only considered when no current static-analysis gaps remain
and the separate session verification/source retention gates are ready.

### App Changes

- `packages/core/src/teaching-workspace.ts`
  - computes absorbed artifact count, investigation gap count, cleanup verdict,
    and cleanup reason for HTML and Markdown source absorption ledgers
- `packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts`
  - asserts generated HTML and Markdown ledgers include the investigation
    summary and cleanup decision fields
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the cleanup verdict and raise `audit:brief` to
    38 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/core/src/pipeline.test.ts -t "generates a complete study session"`:
  PASS; generated absorption ledger HTML and Markdown include `조사 종료 요약`,
  `흡수한 학습 산출물`, `정리 판단`, and `판단 근거`
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/core build`: PASS
- `git diff --check`: PASS

- `pnpm verify:learning-targets`: PASS, `targets: 32`
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm build && pnpm audit:brief`: PASS,
  `iterations: 38`, `allPassed: true`

- No automatic cleanup was added.
- No cleanup decision bypasses session verification or source retention gates.

## Iteration 25 - Offline HTML Search Accessible Label

### Baseline

The generated offline HTML reports included a global sidebar search input with
`id="search"` and `placeholder="검색"`, but no explicit accessible label. A
visual learner could see the field, but browser automation and assistive
technology could not address it by a stable Korean label.

### Candidate Adopted

`offline html search accessible label`

The generated report shell now renders:

```html
<input id="search" type="search" aria-label="리포트 검색" placeholder="검색">
```

This preserves the visible layout and existing JavaScript filter behavior while
making the report search control addressable by label.

### App Changes

- `packages/html/src/templates.ts`
  - adds `aria-label="리포트 검색"` to the offline HTML report search input
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the offline HTML search accessible label and
    raise `audit:brief` to 25 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/html typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/html build`: PASS
- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- CLI study smoke on `packages/core/tests/fixtures/simple-ts-app`: PASS
  - generated `html/index.html`
  - `verificationOk: true`
  - `verificationCheckedRequiredArtifacts: 754`
- Playwright smoke on the generated `file://.../html/index.html`: PASS
  - `getByLabel("리포트 검색").fill("architecture")` updated the search input
  - `#search` had `aria-label="리포트 검색"`
  - screenshot: `/tmp/repotutor-offline-html-search-label.png`

### Not Adopted

- No visual layout change was made.
- No search-index algorithm change was made.
- No source snapshot retention behavior changed.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. The CLI smoke generated a
temporary `/tmp` study session for verification only.

## Iteration 26 - Compliance Audit Snapshot Reuse

### Baseline

`pnpm audit:brief` had become the slowest recurring verification gate. The
script built the same check list for every historical iteration:

```js
checks: checks.map((fn) => fn())
```

Because each invocation audits one current workspace snapshot, recomputing the
same file/string checks for every generated historical report added repeated
scans of large files such as `working.md`.

### Candidate Adopted

`compliance audit snapshot reuse`

The audit now computes the check result once per invocation:

```js
const baseChecks = checks.map((fn) => fn());
```

Each per-iteration report receives a cloned copy through `cloneAuditChecks`.
This keeps report content independent while avoiding repeated file/string
checks against the same snapshot.

### App Changes

- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`
  - adds `baseChecks`
  - adds `cloneAuditChecks`
  - reuses the same snapshot check result for all generated iteration reports
- `package.json`
  - raises `audit:brief` to 26 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- Timed `pnpm audit:brief`: PASS
  - `iterations: 26`
  - `allPassed: true`
  - elapsed: about 4.5 seconds

### Not Adopted

- No check was removed.
- No audit report file was skipped.
- No source snapshot retention behavior changed.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration.

## Iteration 28 - Desktop Historical Quiz Summary

### Baseline

When a historical session was selected from the desktop sidebar, `current` was
reconstructed with `quizQuestions: 0`. The details panel then rendered:

```tsx
current.quizQuestions || selectedSession?.score || "생성됨"
```

That could display a score such as `92` in the `퀴즈` field as if it were a
question count. A Playwright smoke also exposed a nearby issue in the sidebar:
unattempted sessions rendered `미응시점`.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop historical quiz summary`

The desktop app now separates question count, latest score, and unattempted
state:

- new sessions with generated quiz questions show `15문제`
- historical scored sessions show `최근 92점`
- historical unattempted sessions show `미응시`
- sidebar rows use the same score formatter and no longer render `미응시점`

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `quizSummaryText`
  - adds `scoreSummaryText`
  - uses the quiz summary helper in the details panel
  - uses the score summary helper in sidebar rows
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the desktop historical quiz summary and raise
    `audit:brief` to 28 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS
- Playwright desktop UI smoke against Vite `http://localhost:1420/`: PASS
  - selected a historical scored session and saw `최근 92점`
  - confirmed no raw `92` appeared as a quiz count
  - selected an unattempted session and saw `미응시`
  - confirmed `미응시점` was absent after restarting the dev server
  - screenshot: `/tmp/repotutor-desktop-historical-quiz-summary.png`

### Not Adopted

- No backend session schema change was made.
- No quiz loading behavior changed.
- No source snapshot retention behavior changed.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. The Playwright smoke used a
mocked Tauri bridge and did not create a real study session.

## Iteration 27 - Desktop Build App Typecheck Gate

### Baseline

During the desktop session search slice, `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri
typecheck` correctly failed on a `SessionRow.status` reference, but
`pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build` still passed because the build
script only ran `tsc -p tsconfig.node.json` before `vite build`. Vite transpiles
the app bundle and can miss TypeScript errors that the app typecheck catches.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop build app typecheck gate`

The desktop build script now runs the app TypeScript project before Vite:

```json
"build": "tsc -p tsconfig.node.json && tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit && vite build"
```

This makes the build gate match the desktop typecheck gate for app code.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/package.json`
  - adds `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` to the desktop build script
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the desktop build app typecheck gate and raise
    `audit:brief` to 27 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- Package script assertion: PASS
  - `tsc -p tsconfig.node.json && tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit && vite build`
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS
  - command output showed the app typecheck command before Vite

### Not Adopted

- No Vite config change was made.
- No desktop runtime behavior changed.
- No source snapshot retention behavior changed.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration.

## Iteration 24 - Desktop Session Search Filter

### Baseline

The desktop sidebar displayed a `세션 검색` input, but the value was not stored
and the session list always rendered every session. That is misleading for a
learner who builds a long history of source analyses and needs to return to a
specific repo, path, mode, score, or date.

### Candidate Adopted

`desktop session search filter`

The sidebar search box now filters the rendered session list by repo, path,
mode, created date, score text, and wrong-answer count. If a search term has no
matches, the sidebar shows `검색 결과가 없습니다`.

### App Changes

- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/App.tsx`
  - adds `sessionSearch`
  - derives `filteredSessions` with `useMemo`
  - adds `aria-label="세션 검색"` to the search input
  - renders `filteredSessions` instead of the full `sessions` array
  - renders the empty state when a non-empty session list has no matches
- `apps/desktop-tauri/src/styles.css`
  - adds the `session-empty` empty-state style
- `scripts/compliance-audit.mjs` and `package.json`
  - add compliance coverage for the desktop session search filter and raise
    `audit:brief` to 24 iterations

### Same-Sample Verification

- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: initially FAIL because
  `SessionRow` does not expose `status`; fixed by removing `status` from the
  search fields
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri typecheck`: PASS
- `pnpm --filter @repotutor/desktop-tauri build`: PASS
- `node --check scripts/compliance-audit.mjs`: PASS
- Playwright desktop UI smoke against Vite `http://localhost:1420/`: PASS
  - mock `list_sessions` returned three sessions
  - `getByLabel("세션 검색").fill("desktop")` left only
    `desktop-accessibility-check` visible
  - `getByLabel("세션 검색").fill("missing-query")` rendered
    `검색 결과가 없습니다`
  - screenshot: `/tmp/repotutor-desktop-session-search.png`

### Not Adopted

- No backend session-list query changes were added.
- No fuzzy ranking or persisted search preference was added.
- No source snapshot retention behavior changed.

### Cleanup Status

No source snapshots were deleted in this iteration. The Playwright smoke used a
mocked Tauri bridge and did not create a real study session.
